markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ghul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1867)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Legends claim the gods banished a number of evil, greedy, and gluttonous [genies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=50) to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) in the early days of creation. There, the genies transformed into the first ghuls, cruel undead that feast on the flesh of the living. The greedy and gluttonous nature of the first ghuls lives on, cursing them with an insatiable need to consume. Ghuls haunt trade routes, cemeteries, and other locations with good hiding places and a regular influx of travelers. From here, ghuls use their abilities to confuse and captivate passersby, luring them toward dens where ghuls can feast on unsuspecting flesh. Unlike a ghoul, a ghul doesn't devour corpses—only live prey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Ghul.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Ghul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1867)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Genie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=216" />
<trait label="Ghul" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=423" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 106

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); one planar language (typically [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), or [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22))

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 85
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5

**Weaknesses**
[good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 piercing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The ghul takes on the appearance of a Small or Medium [hyena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=270). This doesn't change its Speed or Strikes. Effects or abilities that detect the presence of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) don't reveal the ghul as an undead while in this form.

**Devour Flesh** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghul has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** The ghul attempts to eat a portion of the grabbed creature's flesh. It makes a jaws Strike against the grabbed creature. On a success, the ghul successfully devours some of the flesh and regains 3d6 Hit Points. The grabbed creature becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or drained 2 if the jaws Strike was a critical success). The violent consumption requires the ghul to release the creature as it feasts. A ghul can regain Hit Points from consuming the flesh of a given creature only once per hour; it must consume the flesh of a different creature to gain additional Hit Points during this time.

**Luring Laugh** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Requirements** The ghul is in its hyena shape; **Effect** The ghul lets out an alluring chitter. Each creature in a 60-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), and compelled to move toward the ghul on the creature's turn. The effect lasts until the end of the ghul's next turn, but the ghul can Sustain a Spell on Luring Laugh. If the ghul attacks, the fascinated condition ends for only the creature that's attacked. On a successful save, a creature is temporarily immune to Luring Laugh for 24 hours.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Genie Connections</title>

Though they're no longer true [genies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=50), ghuls derive a number of their abilities from their genie origins. It's said ghuls can transform into hyenas because of a desperate wish the first ghuls made as they transformed. Ghuls retain knowledge of the elemental tongues, with most ghuls able to speak one of the following: [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22), or another planar language.
</aside>
name: Ghul

markdown:
When a great gladiator or wrestler meets their demise, their soul can sometimes linger out of a love for combat and sport. Every grappling spirit manifests a mask that represents the legacy of the warrior they were during their life. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Grappling Spirit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1868) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 107 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +18, [Gladiatorial Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +20 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +19 **Will** +15 **HP** 125 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Warrior's Mask** A grappling spirit wears a mask that allows them to abandon their original form and become a warrior focused solely on combat. This mask is quasi-corporeal, allowing it to be manipulated by both corporeal and incorporeal creatures. A creature who has the grappling spirit [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) can remove the mask as an Interact action by succeeding at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the grappling spirit's Fortitude DC. Removing a grappling spirit's mask causes the spirit to fly into a frenzy. The grappling spirit becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) and can use their extra action to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), or Strike. They also gain a +4 status bonus to damage rolls, and they take a –4 status penalty to AC and Reflex saving throws. If the mask is put back on, the grappling spirit ends their frenzy. --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 2d10+12 negative plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ghostly Grasp** A grappling spirit can [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) corporeal creatures despite being incorporeal. The grappling spirit uses their [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Grapple as normal but can't use Athletics for other actions that require corporeal contact, like [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40). **Submission Lock** ([Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)) **Requirements** The grappling spirit has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** The spirit saps their opponent's strength with a supernatural grip. The grappling spirit attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature they have grabbed or restrained, also adding 4d10+12 negative damage, depending on the result.
**Critical Success** The creature takes double damage. If it's already [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), it falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38). If not, it is enfeebled 2 until the end of its next turn and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature takes full damage and is enfeebled 1 until the end of its next turn. **Teleporting Clothesline** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The grappling spirit has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); Effect The grappling spirit [pushes](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the creature 15 feet in a straight line. The spirit immediately teleports 20 feet to intercept. They make a ghostly hand Strike against the creature. On a hit, the creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), and on a critical hit, it also takes an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage as it's driven to the ground. **Victory Celebration** If a spirit knocks a creature [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), each creature within 60 feet that witnesses the victory is targeted with a DC 26 [_roaring applause_](/Spells.aspx?ID=987) spell. The spirit must spend all their actions until the end of the turn celebrating, and a new medal or belt appears on the spirit.
Lands of Champions While a grappling spirit can be found almost anywhere notable combats are held, a few regions on Golarion are known for their prominence. The Hold of Belkzen is home to orc grapplers who become grappling spirits, while the wrestlers of Arcadia and Iblydos tend to return for one last glorious match. Grappling spirits are particularly common in the Lands of Second Souls in southern Arcadia.
Retiring a Grappling Spirit The means of appeasing each grappling spirit, allowing it to pass on, vary. Some seek one last match worthy of their skill, while others wish to win a major bout in front of an adoring crowd or even suffer an epic defeat at the hands of a worthy successor. They tend not to be malicious, just entirely dedicated to competition.
name: Grappling Spirit
resistance_markdown: all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

Exemplars of undying violence, graveknight warmasters are devastating forces on the battlefield, able to spur allies to ever greater levels of violence. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Graveknight Warmaster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1869) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 110 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +25, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, [Heavy Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=70) (20 bolts), [War Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=46) --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +24 **Will** +24 **HP** 255 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Hungry Armor** A creature that Strikes a graveknight warmaster with a melee weapon must succeed at a DC 31 Reflex save or be [disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) of that weapon. If the creature critically fails, the weapon ends up in the graveknight's space. A creature that hits a graveknight warmaster with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack must succeed at a DC 31 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the graveknight until the end of its next turn, it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), or the graveknight moves, whichever comes first.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a graveknight is destroyed, their armor rebuilds their body over the course of 1d10 days—or more quickly if the armor is worn by a living host (see Graveknight Armor below). If the body is destroyed before then, the process restarts. A graveknight can only be permanently destroyed by obliterating their armor (such as with [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76)), transporting it to the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3), or throwing it into the heart of a volcano.
**Sacrilegious Aura** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it, with a +23 counteract modifier.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** war flail +29 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 3d10+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** fist +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Ranged** heavy crossbow +25 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 2](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d10+6 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of lightning. Creatures in the area take 8d12 electricity damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Exemplar of Violence** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The graveknight attempts a Strike as their armor flashes with sinister power that spurs allies to violence. After the Strike, allies who can see the graveknight can use a reaction to Step or Stride, but they must end this movement in a space adjacent to an enemy. One ally of the graveknight's choice can instead use a reaction to Strike. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour; **Saving Throw** DC 39 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) and can't remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and can't remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight **Phantom Mount** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) The graveknight summons a supernatural mount as per [_phantom steed_](/Spells.aspx?ID=221), heightened to 7th level. The steed has AC 34, Fort +23, Ref +20, Will +20, and 85 Hit Points. If the steed is destroyed, the graveknight must wait 1 hour before using this ability again. **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapon or [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack the graveknight uses gains the effects of the _+1 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ and [_greater shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) weapon runes. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the [critical specialization effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of any weapons they wield.
name: Graveknight Warmaster

markdown:
Faithless ecclesiarchs were powerful religious leaders in life—archpriests, hierarchs, oracles, and divine servitors personally blessed by their deities. They are adept at corrupting the pious and inspiring their fellow herexens, often becoming leaders among their kind. This faithless ecclesiarch once worshipped [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12), god of magic. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Faithless Ecclesiarch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1870) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 112 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Nethys Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Items** defiled [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12), _+1 [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_ --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 **HP** 93 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Final Blasphemy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When the herexen is destroyed, it explodes in a wave of negative energy with the effects of a 3rd-level, 3-action [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell (DC 24). The herexen is destroyed, so it doesn't gain any Hit Points from this use of _harm_, and it doesn't need to have any _harm_ spells remaining to use this ability.
**Inspire the Faithless** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 30 feet. Other herexens are bolstered by the presence of a faithless ecclesiarch. Whenever a herexen in the aura casts a [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell, that _harm_ spell is heightened by 1 level (to a maximum of 3rd). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _staff_ +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning plus heretic's smite **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) - **3rd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×5), [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) **Cleric Domain Spells** DC 24, 1 Focus Point - **3rd** [Cry of Destruction](/Spells.aspx?ID=410) **Heretic's Smite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) While wielding the favored weapon of its former deity (such as a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) for an ex-[Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12) herexen), the herexen's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 evil damage against creatures that can cast [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells. This damages divine spellcasters who follow the herexen's former faith regardless of their alignment. **Siphon Faith** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** A creature fails a saving throw against a spell cast by the faithless ecclesiarch; **Effect** The faithless ecclesiarch siphons the triggering creature's spiritual energy to fuel their own magic. The triggering creature is stupefied 1 for 1 round, and the faithless ecclesiarch regains 1 Focus Point.
name: Faithless Ecclesiarch

markdown:
While most herexens were clerics or clergy in life, fallen champions were holy crusaders that upheld the ideals of their deity while fighting enemies of the church and defending its congregants. Fallen champions are direct in their actions and wage their divine war on a grand scale. This fallen champion once worshiped the Arcadian goddess [Kazutal](/Deities.aspx?ID=41), also called Mother Jaguar. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Fallen Champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1871) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 113 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Kazutal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [machete](/Weapons.aspx?ID=95)_, defiled [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Kazutal](/Deities.aspx?ID=41), _+1 [scale mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8), _ [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 **HP** 130 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Agent of Despair** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 15 feet. A fallen champion emanates an aura of utter despair. Living creatures are [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) while in a fallen champion's aura. They can't naturally recover from this fear while in the area but recover instantly once they leave the aura.
**Final Blasphemy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When the herexen is destroyed, it explodes in a wave of negative energy with the effects of a 4th-level, 3-action [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) spell (DC 26). The herexen is destroyed, so it doesn't gain any Hit Points from this use of _harm_, and it doesn't need to have any _harm_ spells remaining to use this ability.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _machete_ +20 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus heretic's smite **Divine Innate Spells** DC 25 - **4th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×2) **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 25, 2 Focus Points - **4th** [Touch of Corruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=819), [Unimpeded Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=464) **Heretic's Armaments** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) While the fallen champion wields the favored weapon of its former deity, that weapon gains the effect of a [_fearsome_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=731) property rune. **Heretic's Smite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) While wielding the favored weapon of its former deity (such as a [machete](/Weapons.aspx?ID=95) for an ex-[Kazutal](/Deities.aspx?ID=41) herexen), the herexen's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 evil damage against creatures that can cast [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48) spells. This damages divine spellcasters who follow the herexen's former faith regardless of their alignment. **Reap Faith** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** The fallen champion's last action was a Strike that dealt damage to a living creature; **Effect** The fallen champion saps the hope and faith from that creature. It takes 3d6 negative damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save. Regardless of the result of the save, the creature becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) or increases the value of its frightened condition by 1.
name: Fallen Champion

markdown:
A hollow serpent is the dried and shriveled husk of an enormous constrictor snake, animated by fell magic to serve its masters. According to the oldest holy books of [serpentfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=151), the first hollow serpents were raised by [Ydersius](/Deities.aspx?ID=211). The god took the cast-off skins of great snakes and filled them with hunger and hatred, a bottomless yearning for mortal life that could never be filled. In due course, the priests of the serpentfolk learned to replicate their lord's trick, and in the millennia since the serpentfolk's fall, the knowledge has slithered into the libraries of other necromancers and sorcerers. Despite their menacing reputation and great power, hollow serpents are a relatively uncomplicated creation. They were designed first and foremost as guardians, and serpentfolk liches of a suitable level of power are sometimes accompanied by hollow serpents. A few have struck out on their own over the years, usually due to outliving their original creators. Driven by their hunger and dim intellect, they take up residence in remote ruins or deep caverns. Those that study such things believe at least one serpent, perhaps one of the original creatures made by Ydersius, survives in the mountains west of Geb. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Hollow Serpent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1872) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 114 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** +7 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +30 **Will** +24 **HP** 280 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Desiccation Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 30 feet. A cloud of choking, acrid dust swirls about the hollow serpent, draining moisture from any enemy within. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save or become [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1d4 rounds (2d4 rounds on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Divine Guardian** **Trigger** An ally within the hollow serpent's reach is attacked; **Effect** The hollow serpent interposes a coil of its body or tail between the ally and attacker. The triggering attack targets the hollow serpent instead of the original target. --- **Speed** 50 feet, climb 50 feet; [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **Melee** jaws +30 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d10+15 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tail +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 35 - **7th** [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) (drawing in moisture rather than blood) - **Constant (7th)** [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d10+7 bludgeoning plus drained 1, DC 36 **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** Medium, 4d6 negative plus drained 1, Rupture 32 **Slithering Strike** The hollow serpent Strides or [Climbs](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) and makes a jaws Strike at the end of that movement. If the hollow serpent began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike.
Serpent Forms The original hollow serpents were based on the pythons of the Mwangi jungle. The [ghouls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=218) of Nemret Noktoria have experimented with the skins of magically enlarged horned vipers and spitting cobras, hoping to create venomous variants. So far, they have only made hollow serpents of unusually ominous mien, as their venom fails to survive the transition to undeath.
name: Hollow Serpent

markdown:
While the typical snake oil salesperson seeks riches, some medical fraudsters become so enamored with the swindler lifestyle they reanimate to keep living the lie. Known as ichor slingers, these cadaverous alchemists disguise themselves to walk among the living. There they gleefully concoct tonics and peddle cure-alls, charismatically convincing bystanders to overlook questionable ingredients and credentials. Ichor slingers feed on others' attention and trust, caring little for clients' health so long as buyers imbibe the offered medicines. Conversely, rejection infuriates ichor slingers. The undead often stalk, subdue, and administer unwanted medicines to those who snub them. When not selling “panaceas,” ichor slingers harvest macabre ingredients to make dubious new medicines. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Ichor Slinger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1873) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 115 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any one language **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +13, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Items** [Alchemist's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2), [Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), infused reagents, [Snake Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=136) (6) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 65 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Mystery Ingredients** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 60 feet. Creatures in the aura take a –2 status penalty to checks to [Identify Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=44), [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about alchemical subjects, or examine snake oil effects. When a creature first enters the area, if it hears the ichor slinger talking, it must attempt a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) Will save against the slinger's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC. On a failure, it treats its next failed check to Identify Alchemy or Recall Knowledge about alchemical subjects in the next 24 hours as though it were a critical failure instead. This is an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Brew Tomb Juice** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Cost** 1 batch of infused reagents; **Effect** The ichor slinger creates a vial of tomb juice, an alchemical tonic that has the [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=94) trait and becomes inert after 24 hours. Tomb juice has the effects of snake oil, and maybe more depending on the results of the ichor slinger's DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check.
**Critical Success** The slinger chooses one of the following items and adds its effects to the tomb juice: [moderate antidote](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80), [moderate antiplague](/Equipment.aspx?ID=81), [lesser elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91), [giant scorpion venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=114), or [moderate juggernaut mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=94). Poisons created in this way gain an onset of 1 round, gain the [ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=95) trait, and lose any [contact](/Traits.aspx?ID=37), [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96), or [injury](/Traits.aspx?ID=97) traits.
**Success** As critical success, but the slinger chooses from lesser antidote, lesser antiplague, minor elixir of life, [graveroot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=116), or lesser juggernaut mutagen. **Exhume** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ichor slinger is adjacent to a dead creature that hasn't already been affected by Exhume; **Effect** The ichor slinger harvests portions of the corpse to gain 1 dose of infused reagents, or 2 infused reagents if the creature was undead. **Force Feed** **Requirements** The ichor slinger has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) and is holding tomb juice, an [alchemical elixir](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=8), or an [alchemical poison](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9); **Effect** The ichor slinger forces the target to consume the alchemical item.
Unlikely Allies Rarely, ichor slingers can be non-evil. These variants crave validation and might have once been a physician who failed to detect a plague or a similarly disgraced practitioner of medicine. These alchemists might provide unreliable assistance to heroes and innocents. It's even possible that helping such an ichor slinger cure a disease outbreak could peacefully lay their soul to rest.
name: Ichor Slinger

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Iruxi Ossature](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1874)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

[Iruxis](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=15), also known as lizardfolk, are surrounded by the spirits of their ancestors—literally, as they honor their dead by incorporating their bones into the architecture of iruxi settlements. Decades of veneration by their descendants have caused primal power to accumulate within these remains. In times of crisis, these earth-encrusted bones reanimate as guardians of last resort. Iruxis often refer to these undead—spirits bathed in starlight animating bone and rock, fueled by the beliefs of the living—as “the twilight guardians.”

 Iruxi ossatures usually reanimate spontaneously when a nesting chamber is violated or blood is spilled in an iruxi settlement's main hall. Truly ancient settlements might be blessed with a few ossatures' presence at all times: guarding nurseries, reliquaries, and the sacred observatories where their descendants study the heavens. These particularly hardy ossatures are composed of fossilized bone and possess dinosaur-like shield frills, horns, or spiked tails.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/IruxiOssature.png" />
</column>

</row>

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Twilight Spirit** The faith of its descendants protects the iruxi ossature. It takes no damage from [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) or [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) energy, and it can be healed by either positive or negative energy.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +14,
**Damage** 2d8+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bone javelin +11 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing

**Fossil Fury** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effects** The iruxi ossature beckons with glowing hands, commanding the earth to trip up opponents and marshaling the bone walls of its protectorate to lash out at intruders. The floor in a 30-foot cone in front of the ossature becomes difficult terrain for 1 minute. Any square of a wall built by iruxis that's in the cone lashes out, with fossilized claws damaging any enemy within 5 feet of the wall. This deals 6d6 piercing damage with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature attempts the save only once, even if adjacent to more than one affected square of the wall. Fossil Fury has no effect on the ossature's allies and never damages iruxi eggs.

**Terrain Advantage**   Non-[lizardfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=71) creatures in difficult terrain and non-lizardfolk creatures that are in water and lack a swim Speed are [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to the iruxi ossature.

**Totems of the Past** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The iruxi ossature visits visions made of starlight, resembling totemic paintings or constellation diagrams, upon creatures in a 30-foot emanation. Any [lizardfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=71) allies in the area are inspired, gaining a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, and each enemy in the area must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). Both effects last for 1 minute or until the ossature is destroyed, whichever comes first.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Ossatures Around the World</title>

[Iruxis](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=15) dwell all over Golarion, and their burial customs vary with the terrain and materials at hand. Ossatures composed of petrified wood, bones, and amber guard the rainforest-dwelling iruxis of Garund and Vudra. The red-sandstone ossatures of Casmaron's badlands follow their nomadic descendants' travels, erupting from the earth at sunset.
</aside>
name: Iruxi Ossature

markdown:
Honor and broken oaths bind a last guard to a location they failed to defend in life. There they must keep watch until their duties are fulfilled or they are swayed by a compelling call to war, departing to march to battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 42 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Last Guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1875) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 117 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +38, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +35 **Str** -5 **Dex** +10 **Con** +10 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Battlefield Bound** Without a call to war, a last guard can stray only a short distance from the location they failed to defend, typically 120 feet. Some last guards are instead bound to a province, kingdom, or nation, rather than a specific location. --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +36 **Will** +31 **HP** 330 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Weaknesses** area damage 20, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a last guard is destroyed, they re-form after 2d4 days within the location they're bound to, fully healed. A last guard can be permanently destroyed and move on to the afterlife only if they successfully defend the location they're bound to from large-scale attack, or fulfill a call to war.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** fly 40 feet; troop movement **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** The troop chooses one of the squares it currently occupies and redistributes its squares to any configuration in which all squares are contiguous and within 15 feet of the chosen square. The troop can't share its space with other creatures. **Frightful Battle Cry** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The last guards unleash a soul-shaking bellow in unison. All creatures in a 60-foot cone take 7d6 sonic damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). Any creature that fails its save is also [frightened 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 4 on a critical failure). If the troop occupies 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 30-foot cone. **Ghostly Blades** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The last guards engage in a coordinated attack with their spectral curve blades against each enemy within 5 feet (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d8+2 slashing and 1d8 negative damage
2d8+14 slashing and 2d8 negative damage
3d8+14 slashing and 3d8 negative damage **Spectral Charge** The last guards charge, swarming through their foes. The troop [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to double its Speed, dealing 4d8 negative damage to each creature whose space it moves through (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Any creature that fails its save is also [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or drained 2 on a critical failure). The troop can affect each creature only once in a single use of Spectral Charge. **Troop Movement** Whenever the troop Strides, they first Form Up as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then move up to their Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving.
Armies of Legend Last guards are formed under very specific and tragic circumstances. While this last guard is composed of elves armed with curve blades who failed to defend an elven settlement, last guards can arise from any culture. They most commonly form from the spirits of [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1), [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2), [humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), and [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19).
name: Last Guard

At first glance, a horde lich looks like a skeleton packed with too many bones. In combat, the true purpose of these extra parts becomes clear as limbs peel off, expanding and reassembling into another nimble skeletal form. Regardless of the number, the single lich mind controls them all, and if any of them survive an encounter, they can rebuild the whole given enough time and raw materials. Creating A Horde Lich A horde lich trades frightful presence, Drain Soul Cage, and paralyzing touch for the servitor abilities listed below and the Shatter Block reaction. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Horde Lich](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1876) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 121 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +27, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +31, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +27 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** +8 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** [scroll of _teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338)_, [wand of _dispel magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78)_ (6th) --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +27 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) **HP** 250 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, physical 10 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) bludgeoning) **Rejuvenation** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a lich is destroyed, their soul immediately transfers to their _soul cage_. A lich can be permanently destroyed only if their _soul cage_ is found and destroyed.
**Shatter Block** **Trigger** A creature scores a critical hit on the horde lich; **Requirements** The horde lich has at least one servitor attached (see Servitor Assembly); **Effect** The critical hit is a normal hit instead. One of the horde lich's servitors detaches from them and is placed in an open, adjacent space, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The servitor takes the damage from the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** hand +27 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 5d8 negative **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 39, attack +31 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) - **2nd** [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) (×2) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) - **5th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144) - **6th** [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=254), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) - **7th** [Power Word Blind](/Spells.aspx?ID=226), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237), [Spell Turning](/Spells.aspx?ID=297), [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=346) - **8th** [Horrid Wilting](/Spells.aspx?ID=152), [Power Word Stun](/Spells.aspx?ID=228), [Uncontrollable Dance](/Spells.aspx?ID=347) **Servitor Assembly** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) A horde lich has three servitor bodies folded up and attached to themself, usually in the ribcage, on the arms, and around the legs. The lich detaches one of these servitors and places it in any open, adjacent space. The lich can alternatively take this action to reattach an adjacent servitor.
The lich and their servitors share actions and Hit Points, and the servitors use the same statistics as the lich. Spells and effects that target the lich or their servitors affect all of them equally, but they're still treated as just one creature when targeted by spells and attacks. The horde lich attempts only one save against such effects, but they take a –2 circumstance penalty to defend against effects that target both the lich and one or more detached servitors. Whenever the horde lich Casts a Spell, they can choose to have the spell originate from themself or from any one of their detached servitors. If the horde lich is destroyed, all attached servitors are destroyed as well. If a servitor is dealt a critical hit, it's destroyed (and the horde lich takes the damage as normal). **Servitor Attack** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Each of the horde lich's detached servitors Strike, with a –2 circumstance penalty on the attack roll. Each of these Strikes counts toward the lich's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. **Servitor Lunge** **Requirements** The horde lich's last action was a critical hit with a melee Strike, and the horde lich has at least one servitor attached; **Effect** One of the lich's attached servitors detaches adjacent to the target, and the target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by that servitor. **Servitor Realignment** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Each of the horde lich's detached servitors can either Interact or take a basic action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait. The lich chooses which action each of them takes. **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the horde lich's spellcasting action, the lich attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.
name: Horde Lich

markdown:
In their relentless pursuit of more magical might, some liches use their rotting forms to hold magical secrets, hiding precious tomes in their ribcage, inscribing formulas on their withered flesh, even replacing some bones with powerful magical items. The process is painstaking, as the _soul cage_ tries to repair any damage caused by such alterations. Creating A Runecarved Lich A runecarved lich trades Drain Soul Cage and paralyzing touch for the arm wands and summon weapon abilities. The spell tome and related abilities are acquired by giving up one spell slot of each level they can cast. Some runecarved liches might have other special abilities instead, placing [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) in empty eye sockets, reinforcing themselves with armor plates bearing powerful runes, or even creating reservoirs filled with [alchemical poisons](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=9) or [bombs](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6&Subcategory=7) that pose little danger to the lich but are deadly to those who strike too hard with a bludgeoning attack. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Runecarved Lich](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1877) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 122 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +34, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33 **Str** +5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +10 **Stored Items** The runecarved lich can store items inside its body, as detailed below. The lich can Interact to remove or store an item. If the lich's body is destroyed, any stored items appear among their bones.
  • **Arm Wands** ([extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67)) The runecarved lich can store up to two wands in the radius and ulna of one of their arms. These wands can be used as if wielded, and they can't be disarmed. If an arm wand is overcharged, the lich takes 20 damage if the wand is broken or 40 if it's destroyed.
  • **Spell Tome**; A runecarved lich has a carefully crafted tome, filled with magical power, placed inside their ribcage. This tome can hold up to three spells stolen by the Spellstealing Counter ability.
  • **Stored Weapon** ([extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67)); The lich can store a single one-handed weapon of 1 Bulk or less, linked to special runes carved into the bones of one of the lich's arms. This runecarved lich has the battle axe listed in its items, but a different individual might have a different weapon. A weapon the lich wields deals additional damage equal to half the number of dice from its hand attack.
**Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=298) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16)_, [_wand of manifold missiles_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=370) (7th), [wand of _wall of force](/Spells.aspx?ID=363)_ --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +33 **Will** +36 +1 status to all saves vs. positive **HP** 330 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 60 feet, DC 38
Rejuvenation (arcane, necromancy) When a lich is destroyed, their soul immediately transfers to their _soul cage_. A lich can be permanently destroyed only if their _soul cage_ is found and destroyed.
**Spellstealing Counter** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** A creature the lich can see Casts a Spell; **Requirements** The runecarved lich has fewer than three spells stored in its spell tome; **Effect** The lich expends a spell slot of the same level as the spell being cast to counter the triggering creature's casting. The runecarved lich loses their spell slot as if they'd cast the triggering spell. The lich then attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell (counteract modifier +35). If successful, the spell is countered and added to the runecarved lich's spell tome. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _battle axe_ +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 3d8+13 slashing plus 3d8 negative **Melee** hand +31 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 6d8 negative **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 45, attack +37 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=156), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) (3 slots) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) (3 slots) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [False Vision](/Spells.aspx?ID=109), [Prying Eye](/Spells.aspx?ID=239) (3 slots) - **6th** [Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=298), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) (3 slots) - **7th** [Duplicate Foe](/Spells.aspx?ID=93), [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=100), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) (3 slots) - **8th** [Disappearance](/Spells.aspx?ID=73), [Maze](/Spells.aspx?ID=187), [Mind Blank](/Spells.aspx?ID=192) (3 slots) - **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212), [Telepathic Demand](/Spells.aspx?ID=336), [Wail of the Banshee](/Spells.aspx?ID=361) (3 slots) **Consult the Text** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The runecarved lich causes the pages of their spell tome to flip open, revealing an [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell of 9th level or lower of their choice. For the remainder of the day, the lich can cast that spell as if it were one of their spells known for every level from its base level up to 9th. **Drain Spell Tome** **Effect** The lich draws upon the magic stored in their spell tome to cast one of the spells they've countered. This spell is the same level as the one countered, but it uses the runecarved lich's spell DC and spell attack. Casting a spell in this way removes it from the spell tome. **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the runecarved lich's spellcasting action, the lich attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted. **Summon Weapon** **Requirements** The lich has a stored weapon in its arm and has that hand free; **Effect** The runecarved lich summons its stored weapon into that arm's hand.
name: Runecarved Lich

markdown:
A murdered man left unburied in the wilds might return as a being called a “little man in the woods,” his decayed corpse shriveling until it shrinks to the size of a child. Denied the respect of a burial that would smooth his spirit's transition to the afterlife, he is unable to leave the mortal plane and enter the realm of the spirits. Forgotten, lost, and miserable, suffering from unbearable hunger and cold, the little man in the woods is desperate to return to his mortal home and be laid to rest properly. Despite his understandable motives, the little man in the woods has degenerated into a foul, twisted creature. He deceives travelers with plaintive cries then begs them to lead him to a home he barely recalls. He strangles all who can't or won't aid him, including those who lead him to the wrong home or otherwise delay his journey. The little man in the woods lays his victims' bodies out in the wilds, leaving the corpse unburied and exposed. He guards these rotting corpses over years as they shrivel and decay, until each one rises as another little man in the woods. Like any of the actions he has been compelled to perform, creating more of his kind brings the little man in the woods no pleasure. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Little Man in the Woods](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1878) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 124 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); one additional language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Voice Imitation** A little man in the woods can mimic the sounds of a person in distress by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47). The little man in the woods has a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. **Items** _+1 [shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=77)_ --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 **HP** 95 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Ranged** shortbow +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Adopt Guise** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Playing on the expectations of surrounding creatures, the little man in the woods adopts the guise of a Small or Medium living person that matches the voice he imitates. This guise is subjective and entirely in the mind of each creature around him.
A creature that doesn't believe the little man in the woods' voice imitation sees the little man in the woods as he truly is and isn't fooled by Adopt Guise. **Strangle** **Requirements** The little man in the woods has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** The little man in the woods tightens his grip around the creature's neck, extending the Grab, dealing 2d6+3 bludgeoning damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save, and strangling the creature for as long as it remains grabbed or restrained. A strangled creature can't speak, which prevents it from casting spells with a verbal component or activating items with a command component. The strangled creature must hold its breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
To Curse a Corpse A little man in the woods usually begins undeath with nothing, his possessions having been taken, lost, or destroyed. Over his miserable existence, a little man in the woods seizes the possessions of those he kills, so the corpses retain nothing to link them to their past lives. He takes items he finds useful and abandons the rest, perhaps burying them in the earth, tossing them in water, or otherwise removing the items from the area around the corpses he hopes to curse. Thus, even if the corpses he tends are discovered, returning each one home for appropriate funerary rites is a challenging task.
name: Little Man in the Woods

markdown:
Vengeful spirits called lloronas arise from people who died overcome with the grief and shame that comes with the tragic drowning of a child. In life, a llorona may have contributed to the child's death or simply lost the child to a horrible accident. In death, a llorona's pain manifests as a malevolent desire to drown others, imparting the same grief that they share with the person's loved ones. To this end, lloronas remain along riversides and other bodies of water in hopes of drawing their next victim and compelling them to drown themselves. Lloronas look much like they did in life, except they wear elegant funeral attire in undeath. Though stories typically describe lloronas as women, reports from seasoned adventurers include accounts of lloronas appearing as different genders and with a variety of appearances. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 30 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Llorona](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1879) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 125 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** -5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 **HP** 165 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a llorona is destroyed, they re-form after 1d6 days at the site of their death. Resolving the injustice that led to a llorona's death puts them to rest permanently. Alternatively, successfully performing a [_consecrate_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) ritual at the site prevents them from re-forming. --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** ghostly hand +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+11 negative plus funereal touch and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d6+12 negative, DC 31 **Funereal Touch** A creature touched by a llorona must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (drained 2 on a critical failure) or increases their drained value by 1 (2 on a critical failure), up to a maximum of drained 4. Every time a creature becomes drained in this way, the llorona gains 10 temporary Hit Points for 1 hour. **Ghostly Grasp** A llorona can [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) corporeal creatures despite being incorporeal. The llorona uses their [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Grapple as normal but can't use Athletics for other actions that require corporeal contact, like [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40). **Wail** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The llorona unleashes a somber wail. Each living creature within 120 feet must attempt a DC 31 Will save. Regardless of its result, the creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) with the nearest body of water and compelled to move toward the water and drown itself. If there's no nearby body of water, the creature instead remains still. The creature can attempt another DC 31 Will save at the end of each of its turns. On a success, the creature is no longer fascinated. Once the fascinated condition ends, the creature becomes stunned 1.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature receives another Will save only at the end of a round in which it's submerged in water.
Ghost Stories Stories about lloronas are common in settlements near rivers, lakes, and the sea. These stories are typically aimed at children, intending to keep them from staying out late or venturing near waterways alone.
name: Llorona

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shadern](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1880)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

[Goblin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) mortics have blackened, charred flesh and smoldering burns all over their body, as if they'd been burned alive. They are highly flammable but inured to the pain, and they enjoy lighting themselves (and everything else) on fire.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mortic_ShadernImmolator.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Shadern Immolator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1880)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Goblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=81" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
<trait label="Mortic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=357" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 126

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +3

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Smoke Vision** The shadern ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 21
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)

**Consecration Vulnerability** A shadern in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning plus 1 fire and smoldering fist

**Death Gasp** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The shadern draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The shadern gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the shadern is currently inflicted with are suspended but take effect again once they take a breath. Death Gasp lasts as long as the shadern [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 8 rounds).

**Exhale** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The shadern exhales a cloud of thick smoke in a 10-foot burst centered on them. The cloud remains in place for 1d4 rounds. All creatures within the smoke become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. Exhaling ends Death Gasp, as the shadern ceases holding their breath.

**Smoldering Fist**   On a critical hit, the shadern immolator's fist Strike also deals 1d4 [persistent fire damage.](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-248" />
name: Shadern Immolator

markdown:
Aptly named jitterbones, [halfling](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=5) mortics are capable of controlling the density and rigidity of their skeletal structure. When relaxed, they have elongated, rubbery limbs. When they harden their bones, they become difficult to harm and bony spikes jut from their flesh. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Jitterbone Contortionist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1881) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 126 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +14 **Will** +10 **HP** 56 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Resistances** see Osseous Defense **Consecration Vulnerability** A jitterbone in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **Speed** 25 feet; compression **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d6+2 bludgeoning **Compression** The jitterbone can move through a gap at least 6 inches wide without Squeezing and can Squeeze through a gap at least 2 inches wide. When the jitterbone successfully Squeezes, they move through the tight space at full Speed. Narrow confines aren't difficult terrain for a jitterbone. **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The jitterbone draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The jitterbone gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the jitterbone is currently inflicted with are suspended but take effect again once they take a breath. Death Gasp lasts as long as the jitterbone [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 8 rounds). **Osseous Defense** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The jitterbone elongates their bones, causing jagged bone spikes to protrude from their skin. While Osseous Defense is active, the jitterbone can make bone spike and bone dart Strikes (see below) and gains resistance 5 to piercing damage and slashing damage. The jitterbone loses Compression while their Osseous Defense is active. The jitterbone can end Osseous Defense as a single action, retracting their bones back into their body.
**Melee** bone spike +14 ([finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+2 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)
**Ranged** bone dart +14 ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+2 piercing
name: Jitterbone Contortionist

markdown:
Regardless of age, relictners look like ancient [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) with deep wrinkles, sparse white hair, and decrepit forms. As their bodies degrade, so too do their surroundings, with even the finest architecture crumbling to ruins. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Relictner Eroder](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1882) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 127 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), lair sense **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +23, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +7 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +1 **Lair Sense** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) The relictner bonds with a single structure they consider their lair, such as a house, temple, or ruin. A relictner is aware of any creature that enters the lair, and the direction and approximate distance to the creature. If the lair is destroyed, the relictner can bond with a new lair by residing within a structure they own or claim for 1 week. **Items** [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9), [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) [dwarven war axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=62) --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +18 **Will** +21 **HP** 265 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Consecration Vulnerability** A relictner in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**Weathering Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 60 feet. The relictner radiates an aura of degradation. All objects in the area have their Hardness reduced by 10, to a minimum of 0, for as long as they remain in the aura and for 1 round after leaving it. The relictner can't deactivate this aura. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** eroding touch +26 ([Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 3d12+10 acid **Ranged** targeted collapse +21 ([Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196), [versatile piercing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d10+3 bludgeoning **Crumble** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The relictner erodes an object within 30 feet, dealing 3d10 bludgeoning damage to it, with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save if the object is attended. **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The relictner draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The relictner gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the relictner is currently inflicted with are suspended but take effect again once they take a breath. Death Gasp lasts as long as the relictner [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 8 rounds). **Demolition** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The relictner bellows in fury, causing the surrounding structure to collapse. All creatures in a 30-foot emanation take 8d10 bludgeoning damage from falling debris (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The area becomes difficult terrain. The relictner can't use Demolition again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Relictner Eroder
