<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mummified Cat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1883)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Some of the wealthy have their favored pets mummified or wish to use the animal's fractured memories but strong instincts and natural weapons to turn them into formidable tomb guardians. No other animal was so trusted in the afterlife as felines, who were almost without exception buried alongside deceased pharaohs. Though small in stature, a clowder of mummified cats has ended the life of many a would-be tomb robber.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 14

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Mummified Cat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1883)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Mummy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=228" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 130

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +4, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +0

**Con** +2

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** -2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +4 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 17
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 2

**Aura of Repose** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 60 feet. Corpses within the aura don't decay. This aura also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any effect that would transform a creature in the aura into [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) (counteract level 2, counteract modifier +5).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +8 ([reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 15
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110)

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The mummified cat Strides and makes a Strike at the end of its movement. If the mummified cat began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The mummified cat deals an additional 1d4 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-74" />
name: Mummified Cat

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Decrepit Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1884)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

If a mummy has been in combat, endured the ravages of time, or been exposed to the elements, it can be so damaged it barely functions. These mummies continue guarding their tombs until they are too broken to even move.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mummy_DecrepitMummy.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Decrepit Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1884)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Mummy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=228" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 130

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any one language they knew while alive

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +1

**Con** -2

**Int** -2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 (15 when crumbling)

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5

**Crumbling Form** A decrepit mummy is barely cohesive. Once a decrepit mummy is reduced to fewer than half their Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, they start falling apart, reducing their Armor Class to 15.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11,
**Damage** 1d8+3 bludgeoning plus lesser mummy rot

**Lesser Mummy Rot**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The damage from this disease can't be healed while a creature still has the disease. A creature killed by lesser mummy rot turns to dust and can't be resurrected except by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 2d6 negative damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 day)

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-74" />
name: Decrepit Mummy

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ice Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1885)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The cold of high places coupled with strong winds and less oxygen creates an effect that can preserve bodies, mummifying them through natural processes. Many are created deliberately by cults, and a rare few voluntarily transform.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mummy_IceMummy.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Ice Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1885)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Mummy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=228" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 131

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), snow vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any two languages they knew while alive

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +5

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Snow Vision** The ice mummy ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from falling snow.

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +18 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 130
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10

**Great Despair** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 30 feet. Living creatures are [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) while in an ice mummy's great despair aura. They can't naturally recover from this fear while in the area but recover instantly once they leave it. When a creature first enters the area, it must succeed at a DC 24 Will save (after taking the penalty from being frightened) or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1d4 rounds. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold and cold rot

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold and cold rot

**Channel Rot**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The ice mummy can deliver cold rot through melee weapons they wield.

**Cold Rot**  ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This affliction can't be reduced below stage 1, nor can damage from it be healed, until successfully treated with [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or a similar effect. The affliction can then be removed as normal for a disease. A creature killed by cold rot turns to ice crystals and can't be resurrected except by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Stage 1** [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round, then carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 5d6 negative damage plus 2d6 cold damage and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 day)

**Frozen Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The ice mummy exhales a 60-foot cone of razor-sharp ice that deals 5d6 cold and 4d6 slashing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The ice mummy can't use Frozen Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Frozen in Time</title>

Cults that prepare ice mummies first dehydrate the victim, then take them to the top of a mountain or glacier for a gruesome ritual. The mummy-to-be is stripped naked and rubbed down with oil and fat infused with herbs. The skin is preserved for magical talismans or clothing. If the victim rises to undeath, it receives its own skin to wear.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-74" />
name: Ice Mummy

markdown:
Some enlightened members of the [Iroran](/Deities.aspx?ID=10) faith, particularly monks, are so aware of their own bodily processes they can sense their approaching death from old age. Pinpointing the occurrence to the exact hour, a chosen few employ a method of self-mummification. These masters of life and death leave behind their bodies to watch over Irori's temples. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Iroran Mummy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1886) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 132 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any two languages they knew while alive **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Irori Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +21 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79)_ --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +21 **HP** 190 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10 **Aura of Peace** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. When a creature enters or starts its turn in the aura, it's affected by [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) for 1 minute, with a DC 27 Will save. A creature that succeeds at the save is temporarily immune for 24 hours. If the Iroran mummy takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), the aura of peace is suppressed and its effects end for anyone already affected. The Iroran mummy must spend 10 minutes in meditation to restore the aura.
**Robe Tangle** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Trigger** An adjacent creature misses the Iroran mummy with a melee Strike; **Effect** The mummy swaps positions with the attacker, and the attacker becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the mummy's animated sash. The [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC is 29, the [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) DC is 27, and a creature can sever the sash by hitting AC 27 and dealing at least 20 slashing damage. The mummy can't use this reaction again until they've Interacted to reclaim the sash. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d10+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** _shuriken_ +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing **Monk Ki Spells** DC 29, 3 Focus Points - **5th** [Ki Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=484), [Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Ki Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=486), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490) **Stunning Flurry** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The Iroran mummy makes two unarmed Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses, and the target must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (or stunned 3 on a critical failure); this save has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait.
Iroran Preservation [Irorans](/Deities.aspx?ID=10) closely guard the process of creating their mummies. I have gleaned that it involves rituals of fasting and a diet of certain poisonous nuts and teas used as preservatives. The monk must also be in a trance-like meditation at the moment of death. Physical movement and battle strain a dead body, and long stretches of inactivity might turn skin and bone brittle. Many Iroran mummies treat themselves by rubbing their skin with alchemical oils and wrapping their bodies in magically treated cotton or linen. Choosing a suitable resting spot as well as trying to maintain this resting place is equally important. Upon awakening, Iroran mummies often discover they've become a nesting place for birds or bats or acquired a thin layer of moss.
name: Iroran Mummy

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mummy Prophet of Set](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1887)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In ancient Osirion, worshipers completely devoted to the evil god [Set](/Deities.aspx?ID=242) often underwent a mummification process to serve as his prophets after their death.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Mummy_ProphetOfSet.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Mummy Prophet of Set](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1887)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Mummy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=228" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 132

**Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), sand vision

**Languages**
[Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); plus any two languages they knew while alive

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +27, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +27, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** +6

**Wis** +8

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Sand Vision** The prophet ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from particles of sand and dust in the air.

**Items**
[religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Set](/Deities.aspx?ID=242), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +25 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 15

**Great Despair** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. Living creatures are [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) while in an mummy prophet's great despair aura. They can't naturally recover from this fear while in the area but recover instantly once they leave it. When a creature first enters the area, it must succeed at a DC 31 Will save (after taking the penalty from being frightened) or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1d4 rounds. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_spear_ +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d6+11 piercing plus sand rot

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 3d4+11 bludgeoning plus sand rot

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
spear +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 3d6+11 piercing plus sand rot

**Divine Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Penumbral Shroud](/Spells.aspx?ID=582), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244)
- **2nd**
[Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63) (×2), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271)
- **3rd**
[Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Cup of Dust](/Spells.aspx?ID=572), [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36)
- **4th**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256)
- **5th**
[Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273), [Wall of Flesh](/Spells.aspx?ID=727)
- **6th**
[Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) (×2), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86)
- **7th**
[Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=96) (×2), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×7)

**Channel Rot**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The mummy prophet of Set can deliver devastating rot through melee weapons they wield.

**Breath of Sand** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) **Requirements** The prophet's Sandstorm is active; **Effect** The prophet inhales its Sandstorm, suppressing the effect until the end of the prophet's next turn. It then exhales a 120-foot cone of negatively charged sand that deals 7d6 negative and 7d6 slashing damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).

**Sand Form** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The prophet loses their coherent from and becomes an animated pile of sand. While in this form, the prophet gains resistance 10 to physical damage and is immune to precision damage. They can't cast spells, activate items, or use actions that have the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15) or [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. They gain a fly Speed of 40 feet and can slip through tiny cracks. This lasts for 5 minutes, and the prophet can Dismiss the effect.

**Sand Rot**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) This affliction can't be reduced below stage 1, nor can damage from it be healed, until successfully treated with [_remove curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=250) or a similar effect; the affliction can then be removed as normal for a disease. A creature killed by sand rot turns to sand and can't be resurrected except by a 7th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual or similar magic; **Saving Throw** DC 31 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 minute); **Stage 2** 12d6 negative damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), and [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 day)

**Sandstorm** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The mummy creates a temporary sandstorm in a 30-foot emanation that lasts for 1 minute. Creatures within the emanation take a –4 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or be forced to hold their breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). A creature within the sandstorm at the end of its turn takes 2d6 negative and 1d6 slashing damage.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sand Mummification</title>

To keep from eating or drinking during mummification, prophets of Set grafted metal helmets to their bodies shaped like the heads of sha, sleek beasts that were a symbol of [Set's](/Deities.aspx?ID=242) religion. Through slits in their masks, the prophets consumed alchemically treated sands, which absorbed moisture, caused organ failure, and ruptured the stomach to cause death.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-74" />
name: Mummy Prophet of Set

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Obrousian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1888)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spawned from sorrow and hatred, obrousians are undead [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73) who lurk in shallow places to prey upon hated land dwellers. The genesis of an obrousian is a tragic one: some merfolk forsake their aquatic homes when they fall in love with a land dweller, only to be spurned or rejected. Having given up their entire world for another and then feeling that new world closed off to them as well, merfolk can become emotionally overwhelmed. When such merfolk die, often overcome with sorrow and regret, they rise as obrousians.

 Obrousians think of little other than taking revenge upon the land dwellers who specifically wronged them, and then upon anyone else with the misfortune to cross their paths. They can contort their bodies to take the guise of other humanoids, but doing so is painful, so obrousians use this ability only when absolutely necessary to enact their schemes. While they are capable of long-term planning, their underlying mental turmoil simmers beneath any careful preparation, and many obrousians foil their own plans with unexpected outbursts of emotion.

 Obrousians are recognizable as the merfolk they once were before they died, though their bodies show obvious decay and their hands have elongated into sharp claws. Their voices are melodic and entrancing, belying their rotting bodies and turbulent minds, so some obrousians hide in tall reeds or under docks before speaking to their victims, lulling them into false comfort with a pleasant voice.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Obrousian.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 14">[Obrousian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1888)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 134, [Pathfinder #161: Belly of the Black Whale](/Sources.aspx?ID=51) pg. 86

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 36 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Waves of Sorrow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the obrousian's waves of sorrow aura, the creature is overwhelmed by the sorrow that suffuses the obrousian's being. The creature must succeed at a DC 33 Will saving throw or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 5 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d8+12 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 34
- **3rd**
[Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) (at will)
- **7th**
[Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) (×3)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The obrousian can take on the appearance of any kind of Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature. This transformation increases their Speed to 25 feet and might remove their swim Speed depending on the form. It doesn't change their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning damage).

**Drowning Grasp**   When a creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the obrousian, the creature's lungs magically fill with water. A creature that can't breathe water is [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) while it is grabbed.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Obrousian Allies</title>

Obrousians train aquatic monsters like [sharks](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=90) and [bunyips](/Monsters.aspx?ID=62), both to provide cover for their more subtle attacks and to dispose of any evidence left behind in their assaults. Clever obrousians can convince locals that simple monsters are responsible for their attacks.
</aside>
name: Obrousian

markdown:
Onryos are created when a person dies violently in the grip of extreme emotion, such as unbridled rage or overwhelming sorrow. These twisted undead return as beings of pure vengeance, seeking to endlessly spread suffering. Onryos torment those who killed them, but not by directly attacking their murderer. Instead, an onryo hunts down the friends, family members, and anyone connected to their foe. In doing so, the onryo inflicts as much torment as possible on the person responsible for their demise. An onryo's vengeful nature persists indefinitely; not even the death of their killer sates their lust for destruction. Vengeance is just a way for them to spread their pain to others, and they quickly find new targets for their ire. Onryos always wear funeral clothing, often dripping wet or covered in filth. Most of them also display wounds or signs indicating how they died. As they move, onryos seem to pop in and out of existence, making them difficult to track in combat. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Onryo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1889) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 135 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +23 **Str** +2 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +23 **Will** +23 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) **HP** 180 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 8 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 35 feet; flicker, [_spider climb_](/Spells.aspx?ID=299), [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+8 slashing plus 2d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32 - **6th** [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) - **Constant (4th)** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27), [Spider Climb](/Spells.aspx?ID=299), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Flicker** An onryo's erratic movement makes them difficult to target. If an onryo moves at least 10 feet during a move action, they gain [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the start of their next turn. **Onryo's Rancor** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** The onryo's last action was a successful claw Strike; **Effect** The onryo attempts to instill its need for vengeance into the creature. The onryo, with their touch, casts a 5th-level [_geas_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) on the creature they hit (DC 30 Will). On a failure, it's forced to seek and slay loved ones of the onryo's wrongdoer and anyone who attempts to defend them for up to one week. On a critical failure, the duration is 1 month and the creature gains Onryo's Rancor, which it's also compelled to use by the geas. Regardless of the outcome, the creature is temporarily immune to Onryo's Rancor from this individual for 1 year. **Vengeful Suffocation** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The onryo channels their boundless resentment, manifesting it as a tangled clump of wet hair, a clot of rancid swamp mud, or other disgusting mass of matter in the throat of a creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Failure** The creature becomes sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes sickened 3 and the mass fully blocks the creature's airway. The creature begins [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).
Daionryo Onryos born of the mighty can rise as daionryos with greater abilities related to their deaths or their killers, like controlling the weather to unleash disaster on a larger scale. A daionryo's spirit is so consumed with vengeance that even its destruction isn't enough to stop it. Daionryos have the ability to rejuvenate (as [hungry ghost](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1863)), returning again and again.
name: Onryo

markdown:
A pale sovereign is a rare form of undead arising from a terrible curse that afflicts only those of royal blood. This curse drains its victim's vitality slowly over time, almost appearing to be a form of long-term wasting disease that no medicine can ever cure. The royal stricken by the curse sickens and wastes away until they finally perish, rising as a pale sovereign after death. The appearance of a pale sovereign is similar to their form in life, but so deathly pale and gaunt that no one who resists the mind-clouding effects of their aura would mistake them as living. The curse is known by many names across various worlds—the Wasting, Blighted Legacy, Unraveled Heraldry—but its effects are the same. After suffering the curse, members of a royal bloodline begin to die out, their descendants dwindling, until only one is left. Upon becoming the last remaining member of their line, the unfortunate royal undergoes the transformation into a pale sovereign, the power and potential of the entire line channeled into one extremely dangerous undead creature, even if the royal was relatively inconsequential and unskilled in life. The methods of inflicting the curse vary. Generally it's the result of rare occult rituals requiring a spellcaster of great power. Sometimes the curse is used to target a royal line abusing its authority, but just as often it's used by malicious forces against benevolent rulers or simply out of spite by the instigating caster. Mentioned only in whispers and veiled allusions, the curse is greatly feared by royal families who know of it. Be cautious in your dealings, the rumors say, and be careful who you spurn, for you never know what power they may wield. More rarely, a pale sovereign can arise spontaneously if the last of a royal line dies while under a curse or due to a curse, particularly if they died with tasks left unfinished or as the result of a betrayal. Any curse will do in these rare instances, and it's possible that the first pale sovereign formed in this way. Though morose and brooding, pale sovereigns are usually neutral rather than evil. They're very protective of their demesnes and whatever subjects remain to them, however, and attacking a pale sovereign's territory is the swiftest way to draw their ire. If a pale sovereign is ousted from their country's central throne, they retreat to any other holdings they might still possess and rule those. The attitudes of a pale sovereign's subjects vary. Many aren't even aware of their ruler's true nature. Among those who know the secret, some value their sovereign's protection and view them fondly. Others are uncomfortable with an undead eternal monarch, especially if the sovereign has become corrupt or bitter. A pale sovereign's territory is indelibly marked by their presence. Plants wither and blacken, struggling to grow in dry and lifeless soil. Wild animals often rise as undead unless they receive special ritual burials, leading to packs of skeletal and zombified beasts roaming the territory. Such monsters don't harm the demesne's residents but are quick to attack any intruders not bearing the sovereign's sigil. Even the living sapient inhabitants of a pale sovereign's demesne tend to have a drained, washed-out appearance, as if one step away from the grave themselves. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Pale Sovereign](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1890) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 136 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), sense visitors **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +28 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +9 **Demesne Confinement** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A pale sovereign is tied to their demesne, a territory of a 5-mile radius surrounding their home estate. They can't leave their demesne of their own accord. A pale sovereign taken from their demesne can't use any of their innate spells or rituals and gradually withers over the course of a week, turning to dust if they don't return before 7 days have passed.
**Sense Visitors** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A pale sovereign automatically knows when a creature enters or leaves their demesne, though this doesn't give them precise knowledge of the creature's location. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +30 **Will** +28 –2 to all saves vs. curses **HP** 298 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Aura of Charm** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 80 feet. The aura of a pale sovereign is beguiling and inviting, affecting creatures beginning their turns within it with an 8th-level [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (DC 39 Will save). A creature that fails its Will save perceives the pale sovereign as they were in life, not their current undead form. A creature that succeeds at its save is then temporarily immune to that pale sovereign's aura for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet, [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** rosebriar lash +28 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d8+8 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 41 - **6th** [Bestial Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=670), [Cloak of Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=41), [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104) (at will), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) - **7th** [Curse of Lost Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=683), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Project Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=237), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **8th** [Spirit Song](/Spells.aspx?ID=303), [Spiritual Epidemic](/Spells.aspx?ID=304) - **Constant (5th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Rituals** DC 41 - **3rd** [Unseen Custodians](/Rituals.aspx?ID=39) - **4th** [Garden of Death](/Rituals.aspx?ID=58) - **6th** [Ward Domain](/Rituals.aspx?ID=40) **Curse of Eternal Sleep** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145)) The pale sovereign touches an adjacent living creature to send it into an endless slumber. The target must attempt a DC 39 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The target becomes drowsy and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 minute.
**Failure** The target falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), though it doesn't fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) or release what it's holding. It can't awaken with a successful Perception check, though dealing any amount of damage to the creature breaks the slumber. If it's still unconscious after 1 hour, it awakens.
**Critical Failure** The target falls unconscious permanently, falling prone, and can't be awoken as long as the curse remains. **Siphon Vitality** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The pale sovereign siphons the life force from creatures around them. All living creatures in a 30-foot emanation take 6d8 negative damage (DC 39 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). The pale sovereign regains HP equal to the highest damage a single creature took from this effect.
Breaking the Curse The Blighted Legacy curse afflicts unprepared rulers. This form of undeath tends to limit their ambition, but lifting the curse for their benefit can end this limitation while also making them indebted to you. Tales say if the wrongdoing that prompted the curse is set right, the noble will recover their vitality and return to mortal life. These curses originate from anything from heinous transgressions to petty slights, so determining what counts as atonement isn't easy. Often, the subject is a generation or two removed from the curse's origin, ignorant of who caused it or why. After the impetus of the curse is identified (requiring investigation often accompanied by divination magic), the deed can be addressed. For example, a sovereign whose forebears stole from a powerful witch must return what was stolen or make amends to the witch's descendants. If the curse wasn't spurred by a grievous moral breach, but by cruelty or caprice, one must either convince a member of the caster's bloodline to rescind the curse or invent a purification ritual uniquely designed for that curse's incarnation.
Sights in a Sovereign's Demesne
  • A grove of apple trees, with new fruit rotting on the branches
  • Flocks of skeletal songbirds, chirping silently through broken beaks
  • A small pond that reflects the twisted undead form of living creatures passing by
  • Occasional strains of eerie funeral dirges echoing through the air
  • An insular village where no mirrors can be found
name: Pale Sovereign

markdown:
Polongs exist to serve their creators as loyal familiars, spies, and assassins. Their convenience makes them popular among the wicked, as each resides within a glass bottle which normally seems to contain only a small amount of red liquid. When addressed by their owner, a polong appears within their bottle as a tiny, beautiful humanoid drenched in blood. Forcing one to manifest outside the bottle reveals their true shape: a blood-soaked spirit with a hateful visage and sickle-shaped claws. The ritual to create a polong originated in Minata and is known primarily by Tian-Sing spellcasters, but only those willing to practice such evil. The ritualist must collect the blood of a murdered humanoid in a small bottle and perform daily incantations, which can take up to 14 days. Once the polong forms, they drink the blood of their creator every day, most often taken from a finger inserted into their bottle. At the master's order, a polong will travel up to 10 miles away to locate a particular person, possess them, and either slowly whittle them down via possession until they die, or force them to attack anyone nearby until someone slays the victim in self-defense. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 26 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Polong](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1891) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 138 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 30 feet **Languages** any one language known by the polong's creator **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Bottle Bound** A polong is bound to the bottle from which they spawned. They can leave the bottle only at the will of their master and must return to it once every 24 hours to feed on their creator's blood. Otherwise, the polong withers and dies. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +18 **Will** +19 **HP** 100 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Weaknesses** good 5 **Natural Invisibility** A polong is naturally [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) outside of their bottle. They become visible only when forced out of a possessed creature against their will. Shattering the polong's bottle also forces them to assume a visible form.
**Regenerating Bond** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When a polong possesses a creature, the polong's master gains temporary Hit Points equal to the victim's level × the victim's [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) value caused by the possession. These temporary Hit Points last as long as the possession lasts. --- **Speed** fly 35 feet **Melee** scythe claw +20 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d4+9 slashing plus 1d6 negative **Polong Possession** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)) The polong attempts to possess an adjacent corporeal creature. This has the same effect as the [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225) spell (DC 26 Will), except the duration is 24 hours and, since the polong doesn't have a physical body, they're unaffected by that restriction of the spell. If the target has critically failed its save and become fully possessed by this polong before, it gets a save result one degree of success worse than it rolled.
A creature possessed by a polong becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). These conditions don't reduce naturally unless the polong spends an entire day without possessing the creature. For each consecutive day the polong is able to return and possess the creature, the drained and doomed values each increase by 1, to a maximum of 4. If the polong possesses a different creature, the conditions end for any creature it previously possessed. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** scythe claw
Are You My Mommy? A polong considers their creator to be their parent and refers to them as such. Thus, a cleric or exorcist who forces a polong out of their possessed victim often interrogates the spirit for their “parent's” name in order to apprehend the true mastermind.
name: Polong

markdown:
When a living creature is killed by massive amounts of electricity (such as by being struck by lightning or subjected to a magical attack), their dying terror sometimes causes their soul to linger on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). Necromantic energy takes hold of their brain and nervous system, causing these organs to burst forth from the charred flesh and become a raw nerve. These horrific undead float a few inches off the ground in a macabre arrangement morbidly parodying the creature's living form, with its brain at the top and nerves dangling below, crackling with electrical energy. A raw nerve can sense the thoughts of the living, perceiving them as a chaotic jumble that evokes an uncontrollable rage. The undead seeks to destroy the sources of these thoughts, which often means killing every living creature it can sense. Once a raw nerve detects no more thoughts, it grows still, floating in an almost meditative state. However, its fury instantly reawakens should a thinking creature pass too close. Though it's not mindless, a raw nerve can't be reasoned with, and the only way to stop one is to destroy it. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Raw Nerve](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1892) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 139 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), thoughtsense 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +6 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Thoughtsense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The raw nerve senses all non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creatures at the listed range. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 **HP** 150 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** slashing 5 --- **Speed** 30 feet, float **Melee** nerve ending +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d10+9 electricity plus involuntary reaction **Accelerating Inquest** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The raw nerve mentally probes a creature within 100 feet, attempting an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check against the creature's Will DC. On a success, the raw nerve can Step, and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against that creature until the start of the raw nerve's next turn. **Float** A raw nerve floats a few inches from the ground. As such, it can ignore difficult terrain. **Involuntary Reaction** A creature struck by a raw nerve's nerve ending strike must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex saving throw or be unable to use reactions until the start of its next turn. **Synaptic Overload** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The raw nerve releases a burst of mental energy in a 30-foot cone, dealing 4d12 mental damage to each creature in the cone (DC 23 Will save).
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is stunned 2.
Shock to the System A more powerful version of a raw nerve can be created when a creature is struck multiple times by lightning in a short period of time before dying. Raw nerves “born” in this way seem to appear and disappear at random intervals, like a flash of lightning during a storm. At the end of a powerful raw nerve's turns, roll 1d6. On a 1, 2, or 3, the raw nerve is visible, glowing with bright light that makes creatures [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) while within 10 feet of it. On a 4, 5, or 6, it's [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) (as with a 4th-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell).
name: Raw Nerve

markdown:
A pale stranger arises when a gunslinger is slain in a duel by an underhanded foe. Buried with their pistols, the corpse claws out of the grave to wreak vengeance upon their killer for cheating. Sometimes a pale stranger is created when a particularly petty [gunslinger](/Classes.aspx?ID=20) is beaten fairly and refuses to accept defeat, though such an individual must be especially self-delusional to retain the hatred necessary to spur the transformation. Whatever the circumstance, a pale stranger can wield their firearms with deadly grace and efficacy. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Pale Stranger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1893) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 140 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), sense murderer **Languages** any one language spoken in life by their murderer (typically [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Sense Murderer** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A pale stranger knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flintlock pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=192)_ (2) --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +21 **Will** +15 **HP** 155 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Resistances** physical 10 (except slashing) **Self-Loathing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) If a pale stranger sees their own reflection or any object important to them in life, they must attempt a DC 30 Will save.
**Critical Success** The pale stranger is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.
**Success** The pale stranger is distracted by self-loathing and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The pale stranger becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the source of their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of their next turn.
**Critical Failure** The pale stranger becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.
**Undying Vendetta** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the pale stranger's murderer dies, the pale stranger is immediately destroyed. A pale stranger that can't sense their murderer must attempt a DC 11 flat check once every 24 hours to avoid becoming [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31); they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the pale stranger's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The pale stranger gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _pistol whip_ +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+9 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188)), **Damage** 1d6+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** _flintlock pistol_ +24 ([Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401), [fatal d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+6 piercing plus bullets of vengeance **Bullets of Vengeance** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Shots from a pale stranger's firearms deal an additional 1d6 precision damage, or 2d6 precision damage against the one who murdered them. A creature who has never killed a living being doesn't take this precision damage. When a pale stranger critically succeeds at a ranged Strike with a pistol, the target must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). **Dead Shot** **Frequency** once per turn; **Requirements** The pale stranger is wielding two loaded firearms; **Effect** The pale stranger takes aim at a target and shoots both of their firearms simultaneously. The pale stranger makes two ranged Strikes, one with each of the firearms, each using the pale stranger's current multiple attack penalty. Both Strikes must have the same target, who must be within the first range increment of both weapons.
If both attacks hit, combine their damage, and then add any other applicable effects from both weapons. The pale stranger adds any precision damage only once, to the attack of their choice. Combine the damage from both Strikes and apply resistances and weaknesses only once. This counts as two attacks when calculating the pale stranger's multiple attack penalty. **Revenant Reload** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The pale stranger reloads each firearm they're wielding, supernaturally generating new black powder and bullets. When firing these magical bullets, the firearm can't misfire, and the attack doesn't trigger reactions triggered by ranged attacks.
Ghastly Guns The pistols carried by a pale stranger are valuable, but many believe they contain fragments of the creature's soul, making them cursed to slowly corrupt their wielder with the pale stranger's personality. As such, it can be difficult to sell a pale stranger's pistols. Luckily, most underworld markets trade in such goods regardless of their origin.
name: Pale Stranger

markdown:
When a person is betrayed and killed in a way that their voice was silenced (such as through strangulation by a lover or close confidante), they might come back as a silent stalker. A silent stalker is a horrifying, twisted likeness of the person they were in life, with one obvious difference: the silent stalker has no mouth, simply a smooth patch of flesh where a mouth should be. Sound is suppressed in an area around a silent stalker, but once someone notices the quiet, it's often too late. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Silent Stalker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1894) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 141 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), sense murderer **Languages** any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); can't speak) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Sense Murderer** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A silent stalker knows the direction of their murderer (as long as both are on the same plane), but not the distance. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 **HP** 220 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Resistances** physical 15 (except slashing) **Silent Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 10 feet. A silent stalker makes no sound <%END>, preventing creatures from noticing them using hearing or a similar sense alone. This silence stops all sound within it or passing through. The silent stalker and all creatures in the aura can't use [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) attacks or actions that have the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait) this prevents creatures from casting spells with verbal components or activating items with command components.
**Self-Loathing** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) If a silent stalker sees their own reflection or any object important to them in life, they must attempt a DC 33 Will save.
**Critical Success** The silent stalker is unaffected and can no longer be affected by that reflection or object in this way.
**Success** The silent stalker is distracted by self-loathing and is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The silent stalker becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the source of their self-loathing and does everything they can to destroy it until the end of their next turn.
**Critical Failure** The silent stalker becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) as long as the source of their self-loathing is apparent, until they're attacked, or until they see their murderer.
**Undying Vendetta** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the silent stalker's murderer dies, the silent stalker is immediately destroyed. A silent stalker that can't sense their murderer must attempt a DC 11 flat check once every 24 hours to avoid becoming [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) and [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31); they immediately rise again once they can sense their murderer. A murderer who becomes undead does not trigger the silent stalker's destruction until the murderer is finally destroyed. The silent stalker gains a +2 status bonus to checks and DCs against their murderer. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 3d8+7 bludgeoning, DC 33 **Implacable Advance** The silent stalker Strides twice and makes a claw Strike; they can ignore difficult terrain during these Strides if they advance toward their murderer. If they Strike and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) their murderer this turn, they can Constrict as a free action immediately after the Grab.
Silent Superstition One marvels at the way people misunderstand the most basic facts about the magic of the dead. The absolute silence of certain revenants has led ignorant fools to believe they fear sound. People espousing this folly claim one can simply retreat to noisier surroundings, such as a tavern full of rowdy patrons, to keep the stalker at bay. In truth, nothing can stop a silent stalker, apart from destroying them or killing their murderer.
name: Silent Stalker

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shredskin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1897)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Shredskins are flying, flapping animate skins who wander the lands lamenting their fate and feeling hollow and adrift, as if they're missing something crucial inside them. They try to enshroud and seize [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) bodies—either corpses or living creatures, anything to fill that void within themselves. Though many will take any body they can overpower, some know who created them and desire only that body to claim as their own, the truest fulfillment of their accursed nature. Shredskins come from people skinned as inhumane trophies, or who were killed in a gruesome way that left only their skin intact, such as being pulled into a machine and degloved.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**

**Skills**

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
critical hits (except slashing), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), precision, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +11 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 piercing

**Compression**   When a shredskin successfully Squeezes, it moves through the tight space at full speed. Narrow confines are not difficult terrain for a shredskin.

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d6+2 bludgeoning, DC 16

**Control Body** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The shredskin is in the same space as a Medium or Small [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91)-shaped corpse; **Effect** The shredskin wraps itself around the corpse as a new skin and takes control of the host body. While controlling a host, the shredskin uses the host's Speed but its own attacks, and it loses its Grab and Enshroud abilities. Attacks that target the shredskin while it controls a body deal half of the damage to the shredskin and half of the damage to the host body. Attacks that target an area deal damage to both the shredskin and host body normally. The shredskin can release the host body as a free action at the start of its turn. If the host is destroyed, the shredskin automatically releases the body and is flat-footed for 1 round. A Medium corpse typically has 15 Hit Points, while a Small corpse typically has 10 Hit Points. Creatures can notice that a corpse is controlled by a shredskin by succeeding at a Perception check against the shredskin's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC (typically 18).

**Enshroud** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Requirements** The shredskin is [restraining](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) a creature; **Effect** The shredskin wraps itself around the restrained creature like a shirt, seizing control. The shredskin rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the creature becomes restrained until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) and the shredskin can control the creature as described in Control Body. At the start of each of its turns, the shredskin can attempt to Constrict the enshrouded creature as a free action. An enshrouded creature can attempt a DC 16 Escape check to break free (DC 18 if the shredskin critically succeeded its Athletics check to Enshroud).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_6_Geb.png">Under the Skin</title>

No segment of a creature need go to waste when necromantic reanimation provides such variety. A flayed skin has few uses, but enough to keep it from the dustbin. I have several animated skins tattooed with my personal emblem for use as flags. One also wonders if obstinate subjects, under the control of shredskins, might become productive members of society. Worth the attempt.
</aside>
name: Shredskin

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skeletal Soldier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1900)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These soldiers continue to follow orders in death, still wielding the armaments they spent a lifetime learning how to use. While frequently made from the bones of humans or dwarves, other types of humanoids are quite common, such as bugbears, hobgoblins, and orcs. Often, these soldiers wield the weapons most common among their people instead of those listed here.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skeletal Mage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1901)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rarely, the bones of a spellcaster can be used to create a skeleton that retains a limited ability to cast spells. These spells are almost always offensive in nature, and the skeletal mage uses them with reckless abandon. Skeletal mages are often misidentified as [liches](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68), and while not nearly as cunning or deadly, they still pose a significant threat to their foes.

markdown:
Created from the exoskeleton of a [giant stag beetle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=50), this mindless husk can cut a foe in half using its powerful mandibles. The abdomen of this crawling undead is empty, and more than one necromancer has used this space to hide valuable cargo. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Beetle Carapace](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1902) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 148 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +13 **Will** +10 **HP** 90 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 5, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 5, piercing 5, slashing 5 --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 20 feet **Melee** mandible +15, **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** foot +15, **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning **Abdomen Cache** The abdomen of a beetle carapace can be fitted with a simple hinge, allowing it to open and be used as storage. The abdomen can hold up to one Medium or smaller creature, a Large or smaller swarm, or a similar amount of cargo. The beetle or a creature stored in it can Interact to open the hatch. If the beetle carapace takes a critical hit by a bludgeoning weapon, roll a DC 10 flat check. If the check fails, the cache is breached, and its contents spill out of the beetle. The hatch can also be [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) (DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)). **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+2 piercing, DC 24
Crawling Cargo Undead make for the most common passengers inside a beetle carapace's abdomen, but swarms often pack tight inside the creatures. Insects tend to be more comfortable around undead than other types of animals. The following creatures make good options for combat encounters, in which they can be released at a dramatic moment for a grotesque surprise. **Bestiary**: [Centipede swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=78), [scorpion swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=357), [wasp swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=406)
**Bestiary 2**: [Army ant swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=549), [fen mosquito swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=732), [sportlebore swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=816)
**Bestiary 3**: [Bore worm swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1083), [clacking skull swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1307), [hellwasp swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1191), [hermit crab swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1106)
name: Beetle Carapace

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Drake Skeleton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1903)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Moving with sinuous grace, these flying [drake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=36) skeletons are held aloft by rotting membranes stretched between their bony wings. Although just as mindless as other skeletons, skeletal drakes maintain much of the draconic ferocity and raw spite they possessed in life, allowing them to attack viciously.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 148

[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10, piercing 10, slashing 10

**Fragile Wings** When the drake skeleton is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, it can no longer fly. If it's aloft when this happens, it crashes immediately but can use the tatters of its wings to soften the landing, treating the fall as 20 feet shorter to calculate [falling damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=402).

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The skeletal drake makes two Fangs Strikes and one Tail Strike in any order.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skeletal Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1904)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Only the powerful and foolish would raise the bones of a mighty [titan](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=205) or similarly gargantuan creature as a skeleton. Skilled practitioners see this as a waste of a powerful body and imbue them with air walking magic based on those of titans, while the wise know it's nearly impossible to control such a colossus and that it's just as likely to crush its creator underneath its mighty foot as it is to smite their foes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Skeleton_Titan.png" />
</column>

</row>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10, piercing 15, slashing 15

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mountain sword +26 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d12+13 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning

**Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />

**Damage** 2d10+13 plus bone debris

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 31
- **Constant (7th)**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Bone Debris**   The bones a skeletal titan throws are large enough to clutter the battlefield. When the skeletal titan hits a creature with a bone attack, the projectile becomes difficult terrain in the square the creature occupies (or, if the creature occupies more than one square, one square it occupies of the titan's choice). If the titan misses with a bone attack, instead a random square adjacent to the creature becomes difficult terrain.

**Mountain Slam** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The skeletal titan slams its mountain sword into the ground. The shock wave reverberates, dealing mountain sword damage to all creatures in a 20- foot line (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Those who fail are also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Huge or smaller, foot, DC 34

</column>
