markdown:
Most sluagh are reapers: powerful killers subservient to their queen. After they collect souls on a hunt, they dutifully return their plunder to the queen. Though quite clever, they're inscrutable, lacking the whimsy and sociability that characterize many living fey. They do show merriment on occasion though, giggling as their claws draw closer to their quarry's throat. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 29 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Sluagh Reaper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1905) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 150 **Perception** +21; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), soulscent (imprecise) 100 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21 **Str** +3 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Soulscent** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The sluagh is aware of all [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) creatures, [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) creatures, and creatures that died within the last hour within the listed range. --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 175 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, DC 26 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d6+9 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) **Ghostly Swoop** The sluagh reaper becomes [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) until the start of their next turn, and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to their fly Speed. While incorporeal, they are immune to precision damage, and have resistance 10 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)); this resistance is doubled against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) damage. After using Ghostly Swoop, the sluagh reaper can't use it again for 1d4 rounds. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The sluagh reaper deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. **Steal Soul** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The sluagh reaper touches a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) creature or a creature that died within the past 1 hour. If the target is a dying creature, it must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save—on a failure, its dying value increases by 1 (2 on a critical failure). If the target is dead, it receives no save and the sluagh imprisons its soul in a small, grimy sack that keeps stolen souls fresh.
The sack is magically locked. Any sluagh can open it without difficulty, but any others must [Pick the Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) (DC 35) or use [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (5th level; [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC 32). A creature can Interact to empty an unlocked sack, which frees the souls but doesn't return them to life. While a soul is imprisoned, it can't be brought back to life by any means short of a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) or [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196). The sack can hold 12 souls at a time. Sluagh reapers typically attempt to return the souls to a [queen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1906) so she can devour them.
name: Sluagh Reaper

markdown:
A queen sluagh grows more powerful by devouring souls collected from underlings or drained directly through her proboscis. The muscular tongue inside is tipped with a cartilaginous, barbed quill that feeds the queen not only the soul, but the strengths of its owner. A queen conceals her nest deep in a secluded area, such as a forest or graveyard. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 43 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Queen Sluagh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1906) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 151 **Perception** +31; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), soulscent (imprecise) 200 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); voice of the soul **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +31 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 **Soulscent** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The sluagh is aware of all [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) creatures, [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) creatures, and creatures that died within the last hour within the listed range.
**Voice of the Soul** A sluagh queen knows any language of a creature whose soul they've devoured and can perfectly mimic its voice by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47), with a +4 circumstance bonus. --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +27 **Will** +33 **HP** 417 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 120 feet, DC 37.** --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** proboscis +35 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+17 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+17 slashing **Ranged** negative ray +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 7d8 negative **Primal Innate Spells** DC 41 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will) - **7th** [Visions of Danger](/Spells.aspx?ID=358) (a swarm of [sluagh reapers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1905)) - **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=212) **Call of the Damned** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) The queen sends out shriek to all sluagh within 1 mile, summoning them to her. This typically calls 2d4 [sluagh reapers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1905). If the queen is facing foes strong enough to threaten her, these are likely too weak to pose a threat but can assist by fetching items or getting in enemies' way. **Eat Soul** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The queen touches a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) creature or a creature that died within the past 1 hour with her proboscis. If the target is dying, it must attempt a DC 41 Fortitude save; on a failure, its dying value increases by 2 (3 on a critical failure). If the target is dead, the queen devours the soul. Unless the queen is destroyed, the target can't be returned to life through any means, even powerful magic such as [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377). **Ghostly Swoop** The queen sluagh becomes [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) until the start of their next turn, and [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to their fly Speed. While incorporeal, they are immune to precision damage, and have resistance 10 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)); this resistance is doubled against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) damage. After using Ghostly Swoop, the queen sluagh can't use it again for 1d4 rounds. **Soul Theft** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) **Requirements** The queen has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) with her proboscis; **Effect** The queen devours a portion of the creature's soul and steals some of its ability. The creature takes 5d8 negative damage (DC 41 Fortitude save). The queen gains temporary HP equal to the total amount of HP the target loses, including any HP lost from being [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is drained 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is drained 1 and [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). The queen steals a spell slot or training. If she steals a spell from a prepared caster, she transfers the spell to herself; if she steals from a spontaneous caster, she gains a prepared spell of the appropriate level and expends one of the creature's spell slots of that level. If she steals training, she chooses Perception, attack modifier, or any skill. For 24 hours, she gains a +2 status bonus to that statistic and the creature takes a –2 status penalty. The queen can have any number of stolen abilities, but only one from each creature.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage.
name: Queen Sluagh

markdown:
Twisted caricatures of living beings, tormented arise from the remains of those who've been tortured to death. They appear as the mortals they were in life, horribly disfigured by acts of cruelty. Unable to rest, they haunt the sites of their deaths, doomed to relive their last moments of agony. The toxic psychic residue of their death is so great it spills over, afflicting anyone who meets the tormented's haunted gaze. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Tormented](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1907) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 152 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +26 **Will** +27 **HP** 250 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) , regeneration 10 (deactivated by a type listed in endless suffering) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** endless suffering 10 **Endless Suffering** The type of torture that killed a tormented determines the effect of their Tortured Gaze, as listed below. The damage type listed in parentheses deactivates their regeneration and is their damage weakness. The most common tortured deaths are as follows.
  • **Burning** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The tormented perished by fire and brand. The target takes 6d6 fire damage and is wreathed in ghostly flames for 1 minute, negating its [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition and rendering it concealed if it would be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26).
  • **Crushing** (bludgeoning) The tormented was crushed or broken on the wheel. The target takes 4d8 bludgeoning damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
  • **Dislocation** (bludgeoning) The tormented had their limbs repeatedly broken or dislocated. For 1d4 rounds, the target is [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
  • **Drowning** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) The tormented was repeatedly drowned, held underwater and pulled back at the last instant. The target takes 4d6 cold damage and is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
  • **Impalement** (piercing) The tormented was pierced with spears or blades and left to slowly die. The target takes 8d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). (The creature doesn't take the persistent damage this turn, nor does it get its flat check to end the damage.)
  • **Starvation** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27)) The tormented starved to death, denied food until the end. For 1 minute, the target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15).
**Tortured Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, or 60 feet if the tormented is at 125 HP or lower. The psychic agony of a tormented spills into the world around them, inflicting murderous hallucinations replicating the tormented's last moments. A non-[undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 32 Will save or take the effect listed under Endless Suffering. While it has a condition from Tortured Gaze, a creature can't gain a new condition from the aura but can take damage from it again. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** painful touch +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d6+14 mental plus 2d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Scream in Agony** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) Each creature in the tormented's Tortured Gaze aura takes 14d6 mental damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). A creature that fails is also [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (or sickened 2 on a critical failure). The tormented can't Scream in Agony again for 1d4 rounds, but recharges the ability whenever they take damage from an attacker's critical hit or their own critical failure on a saving throw
Tormented In Golarion Tormented are most commonly encountered in places where torture and painful execution is a public spectacle, as if the act of witnessing strengthens the psychic horror that creates such entities. They are most common in Cheliax and Nidal, where public torture is considered high art, though the necromancers of Geb are said to use carefully bound tormented as personal torturers.
name: Tormented

markdown:
An unrisen is a mangled conglomeration of splintered bones, decaying organs, and rotting flesh, created when a ritual such as [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) goes catastrophically wrong. Immense care must be taken, for if the ritual is a critical failure, an unrisen can be the result—as many a ritualist has learned to their horror. Unrisen are barely intelligent, aware only of the agony constantly inflicted by their flawed creation and their resentment for the living. They tend to attack the casters involved in the botched ritual first before lashing out at everyone else around them. Though an unrisen's twisted form is unrecognizable as the intended target of the resurrection, its wordless howls are often disturbingly similar to the deceased's voice. If an unrisen is destroyed before it can rise again, it's reduced to a handful of metallic blue-green salts referred to as essential salts. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Unrisen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1908) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 153 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +17 **Will** +21 **HP** 220 (meant to live, [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** resurrection vulnerability **Meant to Live** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Whenever an unrisen would take damage from [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) energy, it instead heals half that number of Hit Points.
**Resurrection Vulnerability** A creature with a prepared or spontaneous spell that can restore the dead to life (such as [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=29) or [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243)) can expend an appropriate spell slot as a 2-action activity to destroy an unrisen within 30 feet. The attempt fails if the unrisen succeeds at a Will save against the creature's spell DC.
**Rise Again** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) If the unrisen is reduced to 0 Hit Points by means other than fire damage, disintegration, or its resurrection vulnerability, it returns to unlife at the start of its next turn. It has 100 Hit Points and is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in the space in which it was destroyed. The unrisen can't be returned by this ability again for 1 hour. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Agonized Howl** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The unrisen howls in pain at its cursed existence. Creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 9d8 mental damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). The unrisen can't use Agonized Howl again for 1d4 rounds. **Awful Approach** The unrisen reshapes its grotesque form to move swiftly. It Strides twice. Any living creature that can see the unrisen during this movement must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or be [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). This is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) effect. The unrisen can't use Awful Approach again for 1d4 rounds. **Death Grip** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** The unrisen has a living creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** The unrisen attempts to destroy its victim's life force to share the unrisen's fate. The creature must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9). While the curse lasts, the creature regains only half as many HP from [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) effects; if it dies, any attempt to raise it from the dead causes it to return as an unrisen. The curse ends automatically if the creature's doomed value is reduced to 0.
Unrisen Salts An unrisen's essential salts, formed from its remaining distilled life essence, can be used for spells and rituals such as [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243) or [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20), replacing 600 gp worth of diamonds. The existence of these essential salts doesn't damage the soul of the unrisen's source creature, but devout [Pharasmins](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) still frown on its use.
name: Unrisen

Roused rudely from their nightmarish slumber, the provincial jiang-shi roams the villages and graveyards around their domain searching for sustenance to slake their hunger. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Provincial Jiang-Shi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1909) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 158, [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 86 **Perception** +22; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7)_ --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +24 **HP** 130 ([fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), one more breath) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376)) **Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities**
**Warped Fulu** --- **Speed** 15 feet; rigor mortis **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** _mace_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +22 - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (×3) - **4th** [Enervation](/Spells.aspx?ID=687), [Ghostly Tragedy](/Spells.aspx?ID=692) (×2) - **5th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273) - **6th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×7), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=301), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Draining Qi, the provincial jiang-shi regains 13 HP.

markdown:
Ministers of tumult are grandmasters of killing, having cultivated their prowess to supernatural levels through the qi reaved from countless mortals. Many such jiang-shi ministers rule over settlements or even entire city-states, cowing their subjects into unerring obedience. A settlement or city-state under the control of a minister of tumult typically seems, on the outside, like everything is running smoothly and with clockwork precision. Guard patrols, street cleanings, carriages, and more always arrive precisely at the designated time, neither early nor late. To a visitor who doesn't pay any heed to the unease behind the smiles on the inhabitants' faces, the minister of tumult's fiefdom might appear to be a marvelous place to live. But the truth is much more dire. Ministers of tumult keep control and engorge themselves with qi by draining qi from criminals. If the minister of tumult is hungry and there haven't been any serious crimes, they simply dole out this punishment for petty crimes, minor infractions, or simple failures to perform adequately. This keeps the people in check, as there always seems to be a way to avoid the penalty by acting perfectly. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Minister Of Tumult](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1910) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 159, [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 87 **Perception** +28; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +29, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Martial Arts Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +24 **Will** +28 **HP** 190 ([fast healing 10](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15), [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), one more breath) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) [darkwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=376)) **Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities**
**Warped Fulu** --- **Speed** 30 feet; rigor mortis **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Monk Focus Spells** DC 34, 3 Focus Points - **8th** [Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Medusa's Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=739), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When Draining Qi, the minister of tumult regains 19 HP. **Premonition of Death** **Trigger** The minister rolls initiative or their turn begins; **Effect** The minister is attuned to coming violence. The minister uses Stance of Death. **Stance of Death** ([Stance](/Traits.aspx?ID=152), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The minister's qi-engorged form allows perfect body control at all times. They assume one of the following stances.
  • **Bone Corpse** When the minister successfully Drains Qi while in this stance, for 1 round each time the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) creature Strides it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or waste the action and fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
  • **Cloudless Void** The jiang-shi gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and their claw Strikes gain the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) trait.
  • **Sundered Veins** Creatures struck by the jiang-shi's claw Strikes take 1d10 _persistent bleed damage_. On a critical hit, they are also [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).
name: Minister Of Tumult

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vetalarana Emergent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1911)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A vetalarana emergent is inexperienced and ravenous, often caring more for the consumption of thoughts than the quality of the thoughts and memories consumed. These undead gluttons gorge themselves on memories, leaving a crowd of addled and terrified victims in their wake.

 Experienced emergents are cannier than their newly risen kin, stalking their chosen victims and being sure to abduct, kill, or render their victims comatose after draining their memories to ensure no witnesses remain to reveal the presence of a vetalarana.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vampire_VetalaranaEmergent.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vetalarana Manipulator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1912)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A vetalarana manipulator is a gifted strategist, empowered by the memories, thoughts, and talents the manipulator has drained from countless victims and honed by centuries of survival. Certain of their own superiority, manipulators twist free-willed individuals for their own ends as easily as they control their comatose puppets.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

</column>

markdown:
Waldgeists act as avatars of nature that seek vengeance for environmental abuse, such as pollution and deforestation. These undead spirits of destroyed trees form at the site of their destruction or in a nearby woodland, if the original location was entirely razed. They become guardians of the forest, possessing power over the plants within its boundaries. Waldgeists show no mercy to anyone caught damaging their bonded woodland, attacking loggers, [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69), travelers, and invasive species with equal prejudice. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 26 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Waldgeist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1913) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 164 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Woodland Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 100 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Forest Guardian** A waldgeist is compelled to investigate the sound of wood or trees being damaged, such as by being burned or chopped down. A waldgeist that hears such a sound must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or spend all their actions moving toward the sound until they identify the cause. This can't be used to compel a waldgeist to leave their bonded woodland. A waldgeist that succeeds at their saving throw is temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Woodland Dependent** A waldgeist is mystically bonded to a single forest, jungle, orchard, grove, or other similar large, wooded area and must remain within it. If the waldgeist moves outside their bonded woodland, they're immediately destroyed. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** lignifying hand +18 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+7 negative plus lignify **Primal Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **2nd** [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) (at will) - **3rd** [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) - **5th** [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294) **Lignify** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The touch of a waldgeist transforms flesh into wood. A living creature damaged by the waldgeist's lignifying hand Strike must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (or slowed 2 on a critical failure). Further failed saves against lignify increases the slowed condition. Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by lignify, that creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), except they're transformed into wood instead of stone. **Possess Tree** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The waldgeist touches a tree to merge with it, possessing it for up to 1 minute. They can end this possession early as a free action at the start of their turn. While the waldgeist possesses a tree, they lose the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) trait, fly Speed, all resistances, their non-constant spells, and their lignifying hand Strike. Additionally, their size increases to Huge, they gain resistance 5 to bludgeoning and piercing damage, weakness 10 to fire, Speed 20 feet, and the following branch Strike.
**Melee** branch ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)) +20, **Damage** 2d12+9 bludgeoning.
While a waldgeist possesses a tree, any damage that would be dealt to the waldgeist is instead dealt to the tree. The tree has 60 Hit Points. When the tree is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the waldgeist is immediately ejected, and the tree is destroyed.
name: Waldgeist

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Seetangeist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1914)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Seetangeists are composed of the bloated corpses of marine life, encrusted with bleached coral and wrapped in rotting seaweed. Although occasionally created by water-breathing necromancers, seetangeists most often arise when an aquatic ecosystem collapses, resulting in the deaths of countless creatures. Bound by spiritual trauma, these corpses merge together, forming a writhing school of flesh and decaying plant matter that seeks out and consumes what life remains in the region.

 The disasters that lead to the creation of seetangeists are typically caused by mortal interference: overhunting, ecological overexploitation, pollution, or necromantic magic and experimentation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Verdorite_Seetangeist.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=108" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=239" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 165

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) (imprecise) 100 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +4

**Con** +7

**Int** -5

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +25 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 160
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 5, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) 10, slashing 10, piercing 10

**Weaknesses**
area 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10

**Necrotic Runoff** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) 60 feet. A seetangeist secretes foul fluids into the surrounding water, poisoning it. A creature that enters or starts its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or become [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 3 on a critical failure). Creatures that breathe water take a –2 circumstance penalty to this saving throw. As long as a creature remains in the aura, it can't reduce its sickened condition below 1. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** swim 40 feet

**Putrid Assault** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each enemy in the seetangeist's space takes 3d6 slashing and 3d6 poison damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails this save also takes 1d12 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Verdorite Variants</title>

Although seetangeists are the most common type of verdorite swarm, other variants can form in any ecosystem, some of which are truly terrifying. Spells of cold and frost used to combat a [red dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168) in its volcanic lair can inadvertently wipe out entire colonies of algae and snails uniquely adapted to scorching environments. This occasionally gives rise to verdorite swarms that mindlessly grab and burn while being nearly indestructible thanks to their loosely cohesive “bodies” studded with iron-coated shells.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-312" />
name: Seetangeist

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hunter Wight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1915)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Dangers deep in the wilderness claim the life some lone scout or hunter. When they arise as wights, they renew their hunt with equal vigor, but all the living are now their prey. Hunter wights prefer to seek strategic ground, frequently taking residence atop hills or within abandoned watchtowers and keeps.

_composite longbow_ +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 1d8+7 piercing plus drain life

**Drain Life**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the hunter wight damages a living creature using an unarmed attack or their bound weapon, they gain 7 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Actions.aspx?ID=1140). Further damage dealt by the hunter wight's unarmed and bound weapon attacks increases the value of the drained condition by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

**Spawn Hunter Wight**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living humanoid killed by a hunter wight's weapon or claw Strike rises as a hunter wight spawn after 1d4 rounds. This spawn is under the command of the hunter wight that killed them. They don't have drain life or spawn hunter wight and are [clumsy 2](/Actions.aspx?ID=1140) for as long as they're a hunter wight spawn. If the creator of the hunter wight spawn dies, the hunter wight spawn becomes a fully autonomous hunter wight; they regain their free will, gain drain life and spawn hunter wight, and are no longer clumsy.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Bone Arrowheads</title>

A hunter wight snaps bits of their ribs into arrowheads to drain life from a distance. These bones slowly regenerate over time.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-163" />
name: Hunter Wight

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Prowler Wight](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1916)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Often found lurking in the dark, prowler wights wield subterfuge as their greatest weapon. They frequently feign death in old sewers, graveyards, and mausoleums, waiting for hapless victims to pass by before striking.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Medium" />

<trait label="Wight" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=259" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 168

**Dex** +5

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +20 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 155
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Final Spite** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The prowler wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The prowler wight makes a Strike before being destroyed. They don't gain any temporary HP from drain life on this Strike.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_dagger_ +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 2d4+7 piercing plus drain life

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8+7 slashing plus drain life

**Drain Life**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the prowler wight damages a living creature using an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack or their bound weapon, they gain 9 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the prowler wight's unarmed and bound weapon attacks increases the value of the drained condition by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

**Feign Death** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The prowler wight quenches the telltale red glow in their eye sockets, falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), and lies completely still. Until the next time they act, the prowler wight appears to be an ordinary corpse. They have an automatic result of 38 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to pass as an ordinary corpse.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The prowler wight deals an additional 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Spawn Prowler Wight**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) killed by a prowler wight's weapon or claw Strike rises as a prowler wight spawn after 1d4 rounds. This spawn is under the command of the prowler wight that killed them. They don't have drain life or spawn prowler wight and are [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as they're a prowler wight spawn. If the creator of the prowler wight spawn dies, the prowler wight spawn becomes a fully autonomous prowler wight; they regain their free will, gain drain life and spawn prowler wight, and are no longer clumsy.

</column>

When the beloved leader of a military unit is raised as a wight, sometimes the spirits of their comrades return with them, creating a gestalt being of impeccable tactical acumen. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Wight Commander](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1917) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 169 **Perception** +22; (+26 when rolling initiative) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13), +1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [standard-grade adamantine shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=310) (Hardness 10, HP 40, BT 20) --- **AC** 32 (34 with shield raised) **Fort** +24 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 **HP** 220 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Final Spite** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Trigger** The wight commander is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The wight commander blows a battered war horn before being destroyed. The unnerving blast summons reinforcements, raising 1d4 corpses in a 200-foot emanation as [wights](/Monsters.aspx?ID=413).
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+14 slashing plus drain life **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+14 slashing plus drain life **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Coordinated Strike** The wight commander flanks with an ally as long as the target is within both their reaches, even if commander and ally aren't on opposite sides. **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the wight commander damages a living creature using an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack or their bound weapon, they gain 12 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the wight commander's unarmed and bound weapon attacks increases the value of the drained condition by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Spawn Wight Soldier** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) killed by a wight commander's weapon or claw Strike rises as a different wight spawn of the commander's level or lower (most often a [hunter wight spawn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1915) or [prowler wight spawn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1916)) after 1d4 rounds. This wight spawn is under the command of the wight commander that killed them. They don't have drain life or spawn wight and are [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as they're a wight spawn. If the creator of the wight spawn dies, the wight spawn becomes a fully autonomous wight; they regain their free will, gain drain life and spawn wight, and are no longer clumsy. **Tactical Direction** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) Each wight ally in a 30-foot emanation gains the [Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3) reaction until the end of their next turn.
name: Wight Commander

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Husk Zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rather than allow a body to rot, some necromancers attempt to dry-cure the corpse to prevent further deterioration. Combined with special reagents, this process can preserve a rudimentary form of intelligence, allowing husk zombies to act more like predators than their mindless kin. Most husk zombies have no memories of their prior lives, though some keep trinkets from that time, oblivious as to why they value such items. A rare few husk zombies retain most of their former knowledge, though they tend to have gaps in their memory.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Zombie_HuskZombie.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Husk Zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
<trait label="Zombie" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=245" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 170

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Slow** A husk zombie is permanently [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and can't use reactions.

**Items**
[Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 55
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 5, slashing 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The husk zombie deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Sudden Surge** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Trigger** The husk zombie's turn begins; **Effect** The husk zombie has a sudden burst of speed. They are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) this turn and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. If the husk zombie's first action this turn is a melee Strike, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the attack. The husk zombie can't use Sudden Surge again until after they spend at least 10 minutes standing motionless.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Saved Sentience</title>

The rituals to make husk zombies (and withered zombies) require extra steps that involve applying special herbs or tonics to the body before burial. Whether the process preserves the creature's memories is unpredictable at best. For most necromancers, this additional preparation seems like a waste of time as they prefer the total obedience of a mindless zombie. Though most husk zombies want only to hunt and kill, a few try to maintain a more balanced, even cosmopolitan lifestyle.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-103" />
name: Husk Zombie

This deals 1d10+7 bludgeoning damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a critical failure, the creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), and on a critical success the creature is no longer grabbed or restrained. **Horrifying Screech** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The zombie owlbear unleashes a broken, snarling screech that unnerves those who hear it. Each creature in a 60-foot emanation must attempt a DC 19 Will save. Regardless of the result, creatures are temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 round and frightened 3.
name: Zombie Owlbear

Cunning zombie lords command their lesser kin, which they can use in unexpected ways. They have been known to direct or collaborate with [husk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919) and [withered zombies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1922), creating cells of intelligent zombies hiding within the ranks of mindless undead. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Zombie Lord](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1921) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 172 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +10 **Will** +13 **HP** 80 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 5, slashing 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** scythe +12 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d10+8 slashing **Melee** fist +12, **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +13 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×3), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [Sudden Blight](/Spells.aspx?ID=720) **Command Zombie** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The zombie lord intones blasphemous words to gain control over a zombie they can see within 100 feet. A zombie that is [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) and not under someone else's control falls under their control automatically; otherwise, the zombie or its controller can attempt a DC 22 Will save to avoid it being [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6) (whichever has the higher modifier). The target zombie is temporarily immune for 24 hours if successful. The attempt fails if the zombie is the same level as the zombie lord or higher.
The zombie remains controlled by the zombie lord until either is destroyed. A zombie lord can control up to 8 zombies at a time. If the zombie lord would control a number of zombies over this limit, they choose which to release. **Create Zombies** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A zombie lord can create lesser zombies with a 1-hour ritual. During this time, the zombie lord chants and performs an unholy dance over the corpses they intend to animate. Unlike normal rituals, this doesn't require a skill check or Cost. At the end of this hour, the zombie lord can create one 1st- or 2nd-level zombie, or two zombies of level 0 or lower. These are automatically under the zombie lord's control and count toward the limit of Command Zombie. Any zombies created by the zombie lord still count toward the XP budget of an encounter normally. **Shamble Forth!** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Each zombie controlled by the zombie lord Strides 5 feet (or [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climbs](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) 5 feet if it has the corresponding movement type).
name: Zombie Lord

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Withered](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1922)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

If a [husk zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1919) survives for several years, their body continues to dry out, their flesh hardens, and their eyes turn into pale, glowing orbs set deep into otherwise empty sockets. Known as the withered, these zombies are cunning, capable of forming complex plans and even setting traps utilizing their less-intelligent brethren to lure the unsuspecting into deadly ambushes.

They are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) this turn and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. If the withered zombie's first action this turn is a melee Strike, the target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the attack. This monstrous creature can overrun defenses and stomp foes into the ground, making it a terror on any battlefield. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Zombie Mammoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1923) **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 173 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +24 **Str** +7 **Dex** +0 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -3 **Slow** A zombie mammoth is permanently [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and can't use reactions. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +15 **Will** +19 **HP** 290 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) 10, slashing 10 **Severed Trunk** When the zombie mammoth is hit by a critical hit that deals slashing damage, the mammoth's trunk is severed from its body. Any creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) with the trunk is released, and the mammoth loses its trunk Strike and Greater Constrict.
The trunk slithers across the battlefield, still animated, and acts in initiative right after the mammoth. It retains Greater Constrict and trunk Strikes but can't use tusk Strikes, foot Strikes, or Shambling Trample. It has all the stats of the mammoth, except its AC is reduced to 23, it has only 50 Hit Points, and its Speed is 10 feet. Damage dealt to the severed trunk has no effect on the zombie mammoth. --- **Speed** 45 feet **Melee** tusk +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing **Melee** trunk +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** foot +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 2d10+7 bludgeoning, DC 30 **Shambling Trample** As [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) (Large or smaller, foot, DC 30), but the zombie mammoth Strides up to its Speed instead of double.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Clockwork Hunter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1924)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tasks like patrolling the sewers for vermin are mundane, perfect for the clockwork hunter. Its hound-like appearance mimics one of humanity's trustiest hunting companions—with a few choice modifications, of course. Few hunting dogs, for example, sport sturdy metal plating and an integrated crossbow bolt launcher. Over time, clockwork makers have unofficially adopted a common model for the hunter, making it fairly easy to find spare parts or compatible accessory modules.

 The city of Alkenstar owns a few dozen clockwork hunters for the purpose of keeping sewer tunnels free of vermin. Shieldmarshals sometimes drop boxes of extra crossbow bolts just inside sewer entrances, enabling clockwork hunters to resupply as needed.

 Other businesses that tend to purchase clockwork hunters include warehouses, butchers, and bakers—essentially, anywhere rats and other vermin tend to congregate. Though quick, a clockwork hunter's body isn't very sturdy and tends to fall apart after only minor abuse. Elite clockwork hunters are fitted with specialty armored plating, giving them resistance 2 to physical damage (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378)).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Clockwork_ClockworkHunter.png" />
</column>

</row>

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 12

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 2, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276) 2

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
leg +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow bolt launcher +8 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing

**Salvage Ammunition** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The clockwork hunter gathers up any unattended crossbow bolts within reach and reloads its bolt launcher. The hunter restocks its supply of bolts, up to 10 bolts maximum.

**Skirmish Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  The clockwork hunter shuffles its feet and its weapon in tandem. It either Steps and then Strikes, or Strikes and then Steps.

**Target Weakness** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The clockwork hunter takes a moment to scan a creature for weaknesses, then Strikes with a +1 circumstance bonus to its attack roll. The hunter's Strike gains the [deadly d4](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait for this attack.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Clock-Catcher Kobolds</title>

A group of [kobolds](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=18) living in the Alkenstar sewers has created an entire enterprise out of catching clockwork hunters on patrol, dismantling them, and selling the parts back to repair shops on the surface. While the sewage maintenance team hasn't figured it out yet, they're getting suspicious and are about to borrow a [clockwork soldier](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1096) from the grand duchess to kill whatever is down there hunting their clockwork hunters.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Clockwork Handler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1925)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

One of the jobs most commonly outsourced to clockworks is guard duty. [Clockwork soldiers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1096) are often too costly to employ en masse, so municipalities looking to cut corners devised the much-cheaper clockwork handler, a budget-friendly construct sufficient for rat catching, door watching, and basic patrol work.

 Handlers are typically programmed to react efficiently to certain situations such as intruders or assailants. To that end, all handlers are equipped with an [_everburning torch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=255) fastened to their hooded lantern-like heads. Furthermore, most clockwork handlers used in public places have their fists covered in a padded material to ensure they don't accidentally beat suspects to death, though these modifications are removable (removing the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) trait from their fist Strike). The nonlethal approach is considered the safest, as clockwork creatures don't always have the keenest judgement on who is a criminal and who is a customer. A healthy supply of rope allows the clockwork handler to quickly tie-up unruly vagabonds, that they might be brought to justice.

[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
physical 2 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum)

**Weaknesses**
electricity 2, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276) 2

**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
javelin +5 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 piercing

**Hog-tie** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The clockwork handler has a creature [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** The clockwork handler cuts a length of rope from the supply in its chassis to tie up the restrained creature. The creature is restrained until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or [Forces Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the rope with a successful DC 15 check (typically an attack roll or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to Escape, or an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Force Open the ropes; restrained creatures don't take a –2 penalty on this check for not using a crowbar). The rope has Hardness 2 and 8 Hit Points (BT 4).

**Siren** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The clockwork handler emits a loud noise (such as a bell or highpitched mechanical scream) that can be easily heard by anyone within 500 feet. Creatures within 100 feet take a –2 circumstance penalty to Perception checks that are hearing-based. Creatures within 30 feet who fail a DC 14 Fortitude save can't hear anything but the siren, critically fail Perception checks that require hearing, and are immune to [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) effects. This effect lasts until the creature leaves the area and for 1 round afterward.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Customized Handlers</title>

Many organizations customize their clockwork handlers, giving them different equipment, unusual weapons, or just shiny paint jobs to openly display their affiliation with an organization. Shieldmarshalowned clockwork handlers have a navy-blue enamel finish with a painted-on badge, while handlers in service to the Church of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=33) typically sport a pearlescent white veneer and painted-gold detail work.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Clockwork Brewer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1926)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

With the rise of the automated workforce in Alkenstar came the “professional” line of clockworks, capable of carrying out more complex tasks and even interacting with the public. These clockworks were given modules inscribed with information and procedures for carrying out a specific profession. The clockwork brewer is one such example, capable of extracting wort, stirring a fermenter, or monitoring a boiling temperature. As many other types of clockwork professional exist as there are professions; clockworks have been programmed to reshelf library books, swab ship decks, and fill holes in heavily trafficked roads.

 Clockwork professionals made by reputable manufacturers are generally considered safe to interact with the public. Accidents have been known to occur, however, particularly when careless engineers fail to perform regular maintenance checks or when operators program a clockwork with insufficiently specific directives. Flesh and blood laborers forced to work in close proximity to a clockwork professional tend to keep a wide berth.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

**Resistances**

**Bottoms Up** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The clockwork brewer has a creature [grappled](/Actions.aspx?ID=35); **Effect** The clockwork brewer forcefeeds the grappled creature 1 serving of whatever liquid is in the clockwork brewer's tank (typically beer such as Smokeside Sour; see sidebar). The creature is exposed to the liquid's effects, and it can't [breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) or speak until the start of its turn.

**Pre-Programmed Greeting** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The clockwork brewer clearly delivers a disarmingly friendly greeting to a creature within 30 feet. The creature attempts a DC 17 Will save. On a failure, the creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the clockwork brewer's next attack before the end of the brewer's next turn. The target is then immune to Pre-Programmed Greeting for 1 day.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Beer and Ale</title>

Beer, ale, and their many variants are the liquids most commonly contained inside the tanks of Alkenstar's clockwork brewers.

 The following type of beer was originally popular among factory workers in the industrial quarters of Alkenstar, though it can now be found in taverns throughout most of the Inner Sea region.

 **Smokeside Sour** ([ingested](/Traits.aspx?ID=95), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 12 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) (10 minutes); **Stage 2** clumsy 1 and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (10 minutes); **Stage 3** clumsy 2, stupefied 2, and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (40 minutes)
</aside>

A common sight among Alkenstar work crews, construction teams, and mining operations, the clockwork fabricator is a one-size-fits-all construction companion. These clockworks can be programmed to perform up to two simple, repetitive tasks such as hauling equipment, chopping down trees, or grinding up hunks of ore. The modular nature of these units makes them even more valuable—after finishing a day's work sawing planks, the same machine can be unwound, updated with a new modular arm or two, and then re-wound and given new instructions. While it's possible to remove or replace an arm while a fabricator in use, doing so is dangerous and likely to void the manufacturer's warranty, to say nothing of the danger posed to the engineer doing the retrofitting. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Clockwork Fabricator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1927) **Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 85 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +9 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** -5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** 24 hours, DC 19, standby **Items** [Artisan's Tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 50 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 4 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) 4, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=276) 4 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** arm +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** **Requirements** The clockwork fabricator has a creature [grappled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33); **Effect** 2d4+4 bludgeoning, DC 21 **Modular Arms** A clockwork fabricator is built to perform a specific type of task repeatedly, and it comes with built-in artisan's tools and two detachable “arms.” An adjacent creature can forcibly remove a clockwork fabricator's arm with a successful DC 18 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). A creature can Interact to install a new arm with a successful DC 18 [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check. A fabricator has two of any of the following types of arms.
  • **Melee** buzz-saw blade +16 ([forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 1d4+6 slashing plus 1d6 [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)
  • **Melee** crushing vise +14 ([grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184)), **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)
  • **Melee** sledgehammer +12, **Damage** 2d10+6 bludgeoning
  • **Ranged** nail gun +11 ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [range 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8 piercing
  • **Melee** pneumatic chisel +14, **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing
  • **Ranged** towing anchor +11 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing
**Pull** **Requirements** The clockwork fabricator's last action was a successful towing anchor Strike; **Effect** The clockwork fabricator reels in the anchor and attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the target's Fortitude DC. On a success, the clockwork fabricator pulls the target into a free square adjacent to it. This movement is [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451).
Anti-Automated Labor Protests Lately, groups of destitute laborers have been protesting the overuse of clockworks in the day-to-day workforce. They claim workplace injuries are up for those who can get a job, while others have had their positions entirely replaced with automated workers. Though the protests have remained nonviolent, tensions are on the rise, and the shieldmarshals are considering a strong crackdown on protesters.
name: Clockwork Fabricator

<title level="1" pfs="">[Prairie Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1928)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These squat, mud-brown drakes resemble scaly pit bulls with blunt, toothy snouts. The [desert drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=164) is the prairie drake's closest known relative, as both share a strong affinity for earth, can burrow through soil, and hack up balls of dirt that they aim at enemies. But whereas a desert drake is sleek and nimble, flying through lightning storms and siroccos with ease, the prairie drake has all the grace of a chicken, barely able to flutter clumsily up a low hill or over a boulder. Owing to their largely vestigial wings, it's no wonder prairie drakes prefer burrowing over soaring.

 The average prairie drake has a temperament similar to that of a wicked child who delights in tormenting others. They ambush tiny animals from underground and play with their food thoroughly before eating. To assert their supremacy over other scrubland predators, prairie drakes knock down their rivals and attack them mercilessly. They often make grisly displays from the corpses of their foes to intimidate other would-be competitors.

 Prairie drakes build and live in burrow mounds just beneath the surface of their environs. They make shallow tunnels in search of large insects, rodents, and ground snakes. Prairie drakes' presence helps to turn the topsoil and encourage new plant growth, supporting other animal life and making them a keystone species for the environment. Even their breath weapon leaves behind rich “drake soil,” coveted by farmers and gardeners for the potent effects it has on plants. Being seen as “useful” or “beneficial” annoys most prairie drakes, who believe such a reputation makes them look weak and conflicts with their “apex predator” mystique. After they've gotten too much positive attention, a prairie drake is apt to murder a farm animal or destroy croplands only to prove that they're far from harmless.

**Melee**

**Dirt Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The prairie drake spits a ball of dirt that explodes into a shower of rocks and mud. The attack has a range of 30 feet and explodes in a 5-foot radius burst. Creatures within the burst take 3d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). [Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creatures in the area take no damage on a successful save; regardless, once per day, plant creatures can spend 10 minutes in the area to absorb the nutrients and heal 1d8 Hit Points. The prairie drake can't use Dirt Breath again for 1d6 rounds.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The prairie drake makes one jaws Strike and two wing Strikes in any order.

**Speed Surge** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The prairie drake Strides or Burrows twice.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Drake Soil</title>

This nutrient-rich dirt is the result of a prairie drake's breath weapon. The first time they use their breath weapon after a large meal, they the especially enriched soil they spew out is enough to fill a sack weighing 1 Bulk and worth 1 gp to a farmer or gardener. A [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) creature can nestle itself in a sack of enriched drake soil for 10 minutes to regain 1d8 Hit Points, using up all of the soil's additional nutrients.
</aside>
